    Enabling it by default requires either a default ComplexityGate
    implementation (which is itself a dispatcher into AC4 inference) or

The `routing under-specified` error itself is enforced in the upstream
fizeau library at:

  $GOMODCACHE/.../fizeau@v0.9.28/service_execute.go:192

DDx is currently on github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau v0.9.28. The
short-circuit fires when *all* of Harness, Profile, ModelRef, Model,
Provider are empty. AC1 cannot be met purely on the DDx side without
either (a) populating one of those fields from a sensible default
(which is AC4) or (b) a fizeau release that lets the engine's
ResolveRoute see a "global default_provider" hint. Per memory
(feedback_no_local_replace), DDx must always import a properly tagged
agent release, so a `replace` directive is not available even
temporarily.

## What was verified before stopping

  - The under-specified error string is upstream-only — it does not
    occur in any DDx Go file (Grep across cli/, internal/, cmd/).
  - The flag set on `ddx agent execute-loop` already exposes --profile,
    --provider, --harness, --model, --model-ref, --effort. The user's
    report shows `--profile cheap` still failing, which is a separate
    bug from the bead's main thrust: profile is set as a non-empty
    flag default (agent.DefaultRoutingProfile, agent_cmd.go:1563) but
    is being passed through `singleTierAttempt` which uses
    CLIOverrides without setting Profile (agent_cmd.go:1716–1725).
    That single-line gap is plausibly the proximate cause of the
    `--profile cheap` regression but solving it does not satisfy the
    broader bead.
  - Triage gate disabled-by-default is at execute_bead_loop.go:506;
    the gate is a function field on ExecuteBeadWorker. There is no
    in-tree default implementation.

## Suggested decomposition

Recommend splitting this bead into the following children before any
implementation pass attempts AC1:

  ddx-XXXX  Profile flag passthrough fix: ensure --profile/--provider/
            --model-ref reach singleTierAttempt's CLIOverrides
            (single small commit + regression test). Likely closes the
            `--profile cheap` workaround failure.

  ddx-XXXX  Improve under-specified error: when global agent config has
            providers but the request has no routing inputs, point the
            operator at the global default_provider rather than at the
            per-project flag list. Coordinate with fizeau release.

  ddx-XXXX  Tier inference engine over bead metadata
            (kind/labels/file-touch estimate). Aligns with the
            endpoint-first routing redesign.

  ddx-XXXX  ExecuteBeadStatusNeedsTriage status + classify rules +
            close/unclaim policy + tests.

  ddx-XXXX  Default-on ComplexityGate implementation (depends on tier
            inference) + opt-out flag --no-triage-gate.

  ddx-XXXX  Zero-config integration test (depends on the above).

  ddx-XXXX  Doc update: getting-started zero-config flow.

## What a follow-up attempt needs

If a single follow-up attempt is desired, it should narrow the bead to
just AC1 + AC2 with an explicit hand-off: the agent picks a default
harness/provider from the global agent config when nothing else is set,
treating the absence of project config as "use global default_provider"
rather than as a hard error. AC3–AC8 should be filed as sibling beads.

Per the bead's own AC #5, a coarse bead that no tier can confidently
execute is supposed to surface for operator triage rather than burn a
dispatch slot. This rationale is exactly that signal.

        transient failures retry within tier and substantive failures
        escalate. No new test was added to lock that contract in.

## Why this attempt makes no new commits

Two reasons:

1. The work that satisfies AC1, AC2, AC3, AC5, AC7, AC8 is already
   on the project branch via checkpoint commit e2f720e8. Re-applying
   it as my own commit is impossible (already committed) and adding a
   cosmetic completion-marker commit just for bead bookkeeping is
   noise the reviewer cannot meaningfully approve.

2. The remaining gaps (AC4, AC6) are each feature-sized and are
   best filed as child beads per the Step 0 instructions the prior
   pass installed. Per memory feedback_no_local_replace and project
   project_endpoint_routing_design, AC4 in particular should not
   land as a bolt-on ahead of the endpoint-first redesign.

        "routing under-specified". Verified by
        TestZeroConfigWork_NoGlobalProvidersFriendlyError (passes).

  AC3 — Triage gate warning suppressed by default
        cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: L501–L510 — the
        "warning: triage complexity gate is disabled" line is gone when
        ComplexityGate == nil. Test updated:
        TestComplexityGateNilNoWarning (cli/internal/agent/triage_test.go)
        asserts the legacy warning does not appear (passes).

  AC5 — Coarse-bead handling
        cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go: Step 0 size-check section
        prepended to both executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText and
        executeBeadInstructionsAgentText (L1210–L1228 and L1274–L1292).
        Decomposition responsibility now lives in the LLM prompt: the
        agent is instructed to file child beads via `ddx bead create`,
        wire deps via `ddx bead dep add`, annotate the parent, and
        write a no_changes_rationale.txt — exactly the path this very
        attempt is taking.

  AC7 — Integration test
        cli/cmd/zero_config_work_test.go covers the AC's scenario
        (temp project + .ddx/beads.jsonl, minimal global config,
        `ddx work --local --once`, asserts no "routing under-specified"
        and no .ddx/config.yaml side-effect file).

  AC8 — Documentation update
        website/content/docs/getting-started.md gained a "Drain the
        Queue (Zero-Config)" section presenting the no-flags flow as
        the default, with project-level routing as an advanced
        override.

## What is NOT in checkpoint e2f720e8

  AC4 — Per-bead auto-tier selection from bead metadata
        Currently the auto-route picks one profile ("cheap") for the
        whole loop run. Per-bead tier inference from labels/kind/scope
        is unimplemented. Doing it well requires plumbing per-bead
        routing decisions through the execute-loop singleTierAttempt
        path and adding a small classifier (label-based heuristics +
        catalog tier lookup). This overlaps with the open
        endpoint-first routing redesign (project memory
        project_endpoint_routing_design, 2026-04-21) — ad-hoc tier
        inference now will be redone or block that redesign.

  AC6 — Explicit retry-vs-escalate policy verification
        Existing tier escalation in execute_bead_loop.go does run
        (winning_tier=exhausted events confirm it once dispatched),
        but the AC asks for an explicit policy doc + test that
        transient failures retry within tier and substantive failures
        escalate. No new test was added to lock that contract in.

## Why this attempt makes no new commits

Two reasons:

1. The work that satisfies AC1, AC2, AC3, AC5, AC7, AC8 is already
   on the project branch via checkpoint commit e2f720e8. Re-applying
   it as my own commit is impossible (already committed) and adding a
   cosmetic completion-marker commit just for bead bookkeeping is
   noise the reviewer cannot meaningfully approve.

2. The remaining gaps (AC4, AC6) are each feature-sized and are
   best filed as child beads per the Step 0 instructions the prior
   pass installed. Per memory feedback_no_local_replace and project
   project_endpoint_routing_design, AC4 in particular should not
   land as a bolt-on ahead of the endpoint-first redesign.

## Recommended next action

Close ddx-b790449b as substantially-complete and file two child
beads:

  - ddx-XXXX  Per-bead auto-tier selection from bead metadata
              (depends on / aligned with endpoint-first routing
              redesign). Adds label/kind heuristics + catalog tier
              lookup wired into singleTierAttempt.

  - ddx-XXXX  Lock retry-vs-escalate contract: a regression test
              asserting transient (network/rate-limit/timeout)
              failures retry within tier while substantive
              (probe_ok+exec_fail) failures escalate.

README or docs/ entry describes how to regenerate fixtures via scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh when omlx server behavior changes.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T04:14:07Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"ad14ee84ff0faa5b8754f5274ea795fb1511cde7","created_at":"2026-04-18T03:01:36.207143767Z","description":"## Context

ddx-agent v0.3.14 shipped the SSE comment-frame filter that unblocks omlx. Manual dogfooding (2026-04-18) confirmed a single bead executing via vidar-omlx for 6+ minutes with 17,000+ streaming events before the fix caused the regression this bead is meant to prevent: we have no automated assertion that the omlx path actually stays healthy.

Both fresh-eyes reviews (codex + opus, 2026-04-18) flagged: 'missing bead for v0.3.14 consumer-side smoke test in DDx'. This is that bead.

## Fix

Add an end-to-end smoke test that runs a real ddx work invocation against a recorded-or-live omlx endpoint and asserts the attempt closes (or at least produces a closing-status result: success / already_satisfied / a genuine model-side error) without surfacing 'unexpected end of JSON input' or silent stream termination.

Two implementation options:

1. **Record + replay via virtual harness** (preferred for CI isolation). Capture a full AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 trace of a successful vidar-omlx run, store it as a fixture, replay via a test double that emulates omlx's SSE quirks (keep-alive comment frames, reasoning warmup silence, reasoning_content deltas). Test asserts ddx-agent SDK consumes the full stream, ddx work's execute-bead orchestrator records closing status, and bead state mutates correctly.

2. **Live smoke, opt-in** (developer-machine only). A test file guarded by build tag  that makes a real request to vidar-omlx. Useful for catching provider-side regressions (e.g., omlx server ships a breaking change). Skip in CI.

Do BOTH: option 1 for CI signal, option 2 for manual release verification.

## What this prevents

- Regression: a future ddx-agent bump reintroduces the SSE-comment-frame bug or similar streaming defect. CI fixture test catches it before the bump merges.
- Regression: DDx-side change breaks the omlx dispatch path (e.g., adds a header that trips omlx's parser). CI catches it.
- Silent-success: ddx work completes but actually landed a bogus result_rev because of a subtle SSE-frame-dropping bug. Fixture replay asserts the expected final outcome.

## Coverage target

Minimum: one happy-path fixture (successful bead close) AND one error-path fixture (model returns malformed tool-call; execute-bead records task_failed cleanly without corrupting the bead tracker).

## Files likely touched

- cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go (new) — fixture replay + assertions
- testdata/omlx-wire/ (new) — captured AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE JSONL fixtures
- scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh (new) — helper to regenerate fixtures against a live endpoint

failures are reported via result.ExitCode / result.Error\")\n+\n+\t\t\t// The regression signature is always disallowed — on BOTH paths.\n+\t\t\t// v0.3.13 would surface this even for well-formed streams when\n+\t\t\t// keep-alive comment frames were present.\n+\t\t\tif fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorMustNotContain != \"\" {\n+\t\t\t\tassert.NotContains(t, result.Error, fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorMustNotContain,\n+\t\t\t\t\t\"consumer surfaced the v0.3.13 SSE comment-frame regression signature — the sseCommentFilter in ddx-agent v0.3.14+ is missing or broken\")\n+\t\t\t}\n+\n+\t\t\tswitch fix.Meta.Expected.Status {\n+\t\t\tcase \"success\":\n+\t\t\t\tassert.Equal(t, 0, result.ExitCode, \"happy-path fixture must close cleanly; got error=%q\", result.Error)\n+\t\t\t\tassert.Empty(t, result.Error, \"happy-path must produce no error\")\n+\t\t\t\tif want := fix.Meta.Expected.ContentContains; want != \"\" {\n+\t\t\t\t\tassert.Contains(t, result.Output, want,\n+\t\t\t\t\t\t\"happy-path must assemble content from post-keep-alive data frames\")\n+\t\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tcase \"error\":\n+\t\t\t\tassert.Equal(t, 1, result.ExitCode, \"error-path fixture must report failure via ExitCode=1\")\n+\t\t\t\tassert.NotEmpty(t, result.Error, \"error-path must surface a non-empty error\")\n+\t\t\t\tif want := fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorContains; 00000000..1f3cbdfa\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/README.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,61 @@\n+# omlx SSE Wire Fixtures\n+\n+These fixtures drive `cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go` — the consumer-side\n+end-to-end smoke test that guards `ddx work` against regressions on the\n+`ddx-agent → vidar-omlx` dispatch path. Context: bead **ddx-bbb2d177**, which\n+was filed after v0.3.14 shipped the `sseCommentFilter` fix for bead\n+**agent-f237e07b** (upstream).\n+\n+## File format\n+\n+Each fixture is a JSONL file with two record kinds:\n+\n+- First line: `{\"kind\":\"meta\", ...}` — describes the capture and the assertion\n+  shape the replay test will enforce (expected status, substrings that must or\n+  must not appear in the assembled content / error).\n+- Remaining lines: `{\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":\"<raw SSE body>\"}` — one frame per\n+  record, written verbatim (including `\\n\\n` terminators) to the test HTTP\n+  response in arrival order.\n+\n+Frames include both `: keep-alive` SSE comment frames (the exact shape that\n+triggered the v0.3.13 \"unexpected end of JSON input\" regression) and normal\n+`data: {...}` chunks.\n+\n+## When to regenerate\n+\n+Regenerate fixtures only when real omlx server behavior changes in a way the\n+existing captures no longer represent — for example, a new reasoning-phase\n+frame shape, a header shift, or a chunk-boundary change. Do NOT regenerate\n+just to refresh timestamps.\n+\n+## How to regenerate\n+\n+Use `scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh`, which wraps `ddx agent run` with\n+`AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1` (shipped in ddx-agent v0.3.14) so the full\n+stream is captured to a sidecar JSONL, then reshapes it into the fixture\n+format:\n+\n+```bash\n+scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh \\\n+  --endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1 \\\n+  --model    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit \\\n+  --prompt   \"Reply with the word ready.\" \\\n+  --name     happy-path \\\n+  --status   success \\\n+  --out      cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl\n+```\n+\n+After the script writes the fixture, hand-edit the `meta.expected` block to\n+pin the `content_contains` / `error_contains` / `finish_reason` assertions for\n+the capture you just took — the script cannot infer those from the wire\n+bytes alone.\n+\n+## Why fixtures live here instead of the upstream agent repo\n+\n+The agent repo (`DocumentDrivenDX/agent`) has its own provider-level SSE\n+tests in `provider/openai/openai_test.go`. Those guard the SDK's handling of\n+SSE framing in isolation. The fixtures in *this* directory guard the DDx\n+consumer — `ddx work` → `Runner.RunAgent` → `agentlib.Run` — against both\n+SDK regressions (a bad ddx-agent bump) and DDx-side regressions (a header or\n+request-shape change that trips omlx's parser). The two layers cover\n+different failure modes and both matter.\ndiff --git a/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/error_path.jsonl b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/error_path.jsonl\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..3dd1154f\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/error_path.jsonl\n@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@\n+{\"kind\":\"meta\",\"name\":\"error-path\",\"description\":\"omlx-style server sends role delta, a reasoning chunk, then a malformed JSON data frame. b/scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh\n@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@\n+#!/usr/bin/env bash\n+# capture-omlx-fixture.sh — regenerate a testdata/omlx-wire fixture from a\n+# live omlx endpoint. Wraps `ddx agent run` with AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1\n+# (shipped in ddx-agent v0.3.14 — bead agent-f237e07b) and reshapes the wire\n+# dump into the fixture JSONL format the consumer-side smoke test expects.\n+#\n+# Usage:\n+#   scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh \\\n+#     --endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1 \\\n+#     --model    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit \\\n+#     --prompt   \"Reply with the word ready.\" \\\n+#     --out      cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl\n+#\n+# Required tools: ddx (v0.6.0-alpha22+), jq.\n+#\n+# What the script does:\n+#   1. Runs an isolated ddx agent invocation against the given omlx endpoint\n+#      with AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 so the whole\n+#      SSE stream is captured to a sidecar wire file.\n+#   2. Extracts every `dir==\"response\"` body chunk in arrival order and emits\n+#      one {\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":...} record per chunk.\n+#   3. Prepends a {\"kind\":\"meta\",...} record with the endpoint, model, prompt,\n+#      and a user-supplied expected outcome so the replay test knows what to\n+#      assert.\n+#\n+# Fixtures are checked in. Regenerate only when the omlx server changes wire\n+# behavior (new frame type, header shift, chunk-size change) in a way the\n+# existing fixtures no longer represent.\n+\n+set -euo pipefail\n+\n+endpoint=\"\"\n+model=\"\"\n+prompt=\"\"\n+out=\"\"\n+name=\"\"\n+status=\"success\"\n+\n+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do\n+  case \"$1\" in\n+    --endpoint) endpoint=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --model)    model=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --prompt)   prompt=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --out)      out=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --name)     name=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --status)   status=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;  # success | error\n+    -h|--help)\n+      sed -n '2,28p' \"$0\"\n+      exit 0\n+      ;;\n+    *) echo \"unknown arg: $1\" >&2; exit 2 ;;\n+  esac\n+done\n+\n+for v in endpoint model prompt out; do\n+  if [[ -z \"${!v}\" ]]; then\n+    echo \"missing required --$v\" >&2\n+    exit 2\n+  fi\n+done\n+\n+command -v jq >/dev/null || { echo \"jq is required\" >&2; exit 2; }\n+command -v ddx >/dev/null || { echo \"ddx is required\" >&2; exit 2; }\n+\n+wire_file=\"$(mktemp -t omlx-wire.XXXXXX.jsonl)\"\n+trap 'rm -f \"$wire_file\"' EXIT\n+\n+echo \"capturing wire dump from $endpoint → $wire_file\" >&2\n+\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 \\\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 \\\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_FILE=\"$wire_file\" \\\n+ddx agent run \\\n+  --harness agent \\\n+  --provider vidar-omlx \\\n+  --model \"$model\" \\\n+  --prompt-text \"$prompt\" \\\n+  --timeout 120s \\\n+  >/dev/null || {\n+    echo \"ddx agent run failed — fixture capture incomplete\" >&2\n+    echo \"wire dump (may still be useful): $wire_file\" >&2\n+    exit 1\n+  }\n+\n+[[ -z \"$name\" ]] && name=\"$(basename \"$out\" .jsonl)\"\n+\n+tmp_out=\"$(mktemp -t omlx-fixture.XXXXXX.jsonl)\"\n+trap 'rm -f \"$wire_file\" \"$tmp_out\"' EXIT\n+\n+# Meta record — expected outcome is a hint for the replay test. Authors may\n+# need to hand-edit content_contains / error_contains after inspecting the\n+# captured body.\n+jq -cn \\\n+  --arg name \"$name\" \\\n+  --arg desc \"Captured $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) from $endpoint model=$model prompt=$prompt\" \\\n+  --arg status \"$status\" \\\n+  '{kind:\"meta\", name:$name, description:$desc,\n+    expected:{status:$status, error_must_not_contain:\"unexpected end of JSON input\"}}' \\\n+  >> \"$tmp_out\"\n+\n+# Frame records — every response chunk in capture order. AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE\n+# with STREAM_FULL=1 emits one {\"dir\":\"response\",\"body\":\"…\"} record per read.\n+jq -c 'select(.dir==\"response\" and (.body|type==\"string\") and (.body|length>0))\n+       | {kind:\"frame\", body:.body}' \\\n+  \"$wire_file\" >> \"$tmp_out\"\n+\n+mv \"$tmp_out\" \"$out\"\n+echo \"wrote $(wc -l < \"$out\") records to $out\" >&2","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"51e05227-4794-4fdf-b297-f2720148e2a0","uuid":"ad85bb2f-b87a-4555-85d6-b2e11af2638d","timestamp":"2026-04-18T04:31:26.286Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"diff --git a/cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..3438eff3\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go\n@@ -0,0 +1,230 @@\n+package agent\n+\n+import (\n+\t\"bufio\"\n+\t\"encoding/json\"\n+\t\"io\"\n+\t\"net/http\"\n+\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n+\t\"os\"\n+\t\"path/filepath\"\n+\t\"strings\"\n+\t\"testing\"\n+\t\"time\"\n+\n+\t\"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent/provider/openai\"\n+\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert\"\n+\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n+)\n+\n+// omlxFixture is the parsed representation of a testdata/omlx-wire/*.jsonl file.\n+// Each fixture captures a recorded vidar-omlx SSE response plus the outcome the\n+// consumer must produce when it replays. Fixtures are authored either by hand\n+// (the checked-in happy/error paths) or regenerated by\n+// scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh against a live omlx endpoint.\n+type omlxFixture struct {\n+\tMeta   omlxFixtureMeta\n+\tFrames []string // raw SSE bodies, written verbatim in order\n+}\n+\n+type omlxFixtureMeta struct {\n+\tName        string              `json:\"name\"`\n+\tDescription string              `json:\"description\"`\n+\tExpected    omlxFixtureExpected `json:\"expected\"`\n+}\n+\n+type omlxFixtureExpected struct {\n+\t// Status is \"success\" or \"error\".\n+\tStatus string `json:\"status\"`\n+\t// ErrorMustNotContain is a substring that the stream error — if any — must\n+\t// not contain. This is the regression guard: \"unexpected end of JSON input\"\n+\t// is the exact signature of the v0.3.13 SSE comment-frame defect (bead\n+\t// agent-f237e07b) that v0.3.14's sseCommentFilter fixed.\n+\tErrorMustNotContain string `json:\"error_must_not_contain\"`\n+\t// ContentContains is a substring the assembled content must contain on the\n+\t// happy path.\n+\tContentContains string `json:\"content_contains\"`\n+\t// FinishReason is the expected finish reason on the happy path.\n+\tFinishReason string `json:\"finish_reason\"`\n+\t// ErrorContains is a substring the surfaced error must contain on the\n+\t// error path — asserts the error is propagated rather than swallowed.\n+\tErrorContains string `json:\"error_contains\"`\n+}\n+\n+// loadOMLXFixture reads a JSONL fixture from testdata/omlx-wire/. The first\n+// line is the {\"kind\":\"meta\", ...} record; remaining {\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":...}\n+// lines are the SSE bodies to stream in order.\n+func loadOMLXFixture(t *testing.T, path string) omlxFixture {\n+\tt.Helper()\n+\tf, err := os.Open(path) // #nosec G304 -- test fixtures are checked in.\n+\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"open fixture %s\", path)\n+\tt.Cleanup(func() { _ = f.Close() })\n+\n+\tvar fix omlxFixture\n+\tscanner := bufio.NewScanner(f)\n+\t// Default 64KB scanner buffer is tight for long reasoning lines.\n+\tscanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 64*1024), 1024*1024)\n+\tfirst := true\n+\tfor scanner.Scan() {\n+\t\tline := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())\n+\t\tif line == \"\" {\n+\t\t\tcontinue\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tvar record struct {\n+\t\t\tKind string          `json:\"kind\"`\n+\t\t\tBody string          `json:\"body\"`\n+\t\t\tRaw  json.RawMessage `json:\"-\"`\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &record); err != nil {\n+\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"parsing fixture line in %s: %v\", path, err)\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tswitch record.Kind {\n+\t\tcase \"meta\":\n+\t\t\tif !first {\n+\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"fixture %s: meta record must be the first non-empty line\", path)\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &fix.Meta); err != nil {\n+\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"parsing meta in %s: %v\", path, err)\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\tcase \"frame\":\n+\t\t\tfix.Frames = append(fix.Frames, record.Body)\n+\t\tdefault:\n+\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"fixture %s: unknown record kind %q\", path, record.Kind)\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tfirst = false\n+\t}\n+\trequire.NoError(t, scanner.Err(), \"scanning fixture %s\", path)\n+\trequire.NotEmpty(t, fix.Meta.Name, \"fixture %s: missing meta.name\", path)\n+\trequire.NotEmpty(t, fix.Frames, \"fixture %s: no frame records\", path)\n+\treturn fix\n+}\n+\n+// newOMLXReplayServer stands up an httptest.Server that behaves like a vidar-omlx\n+// /v1/chat/completions endpoint by replaying the fixture's frames verbatim,\n+// with a short inter-frame flush so the consumer's SSE decoder sees per-chunk\n+// arrivals rather than one batched blob.\n+func newOMLXReplayServer(t *testing.T, frames []string) *httptest.Server {\n+\tt.Helper()\n+\tsrv := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n+\t\t// Match omlx's advertised Content-Type exactly — the SSE comment-frame\n+\t\t// middleware in provider/openai activates only when Content-Type starts\n+\t\t// with text/event-stream.\n+\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"text/event-stream; charset=utf-8\")\n+\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-cache\")\n+\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Transfer-Encoding\", \"chunked\")\n+\t\tflusher, _ := w.(http.Flusher)\n+\t\tfor _, body := range frames {\n+\t\t\tif _, err := io.WriteString(w, body); err != nil {\n+\t\t\t\treturn\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tif flusher != nil {\n+\t\t\t\tflusher.Flush()\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\t// Small delay to reproduce per-frame arrival; long enough that the\n+\t\t\t// decoder cannot batch frames, short enough that the test stays\n+\t\t\t// under a second total.\n+\t\t\ttime.Sleep(5 * time.Millisecond)\n+\t\t}\n+\t}))\n+\tt.Cleanup(srv.Close)\n+\treturn srv\n+}\n+\n+// TestOMLXFixtureReplay_ConsumerEndToEnd drives a full ddx work orchestration\n+// path (Runner.RunAgent → agentlib.Run → openai.Provider.ChatStream) against an\n+// httptest double that replays recorded omlx SSE wire traces. This is the\n+// consumer-side smoke test for ddx-agent v0.3.14 — see bead ddx-bbb2d177.\n+//\n+// What this guards against:\n+//\n+//  1. A future ddx-agent bump reintroducing the SSE comment-frame bug (bead\n+//     agent-f237e07b): the happy-path fixture includes `: keep-alive\\n\\n`\n+//     frames between data frames and must NOT surface \"unexpected end of\n+//     JSON input\".\n+//  2. A DDx-side change breaking the omlx dispatch path (e.g., a header or\n+//     request-shape change that trips omlx's parser).\n+//  3. A silent-success regression where the stream is consumed without error\n+//     but the assembled content is empty or malformed — the happy-path assertion\n+//     pins both the final content and finish_reason.\n+//  4. Error-path regressions where a real model-side failure is swallowed by\n+//     the consumer — the error-path fixture emits a malformed data frame and\n+//     asserts the consumer surfaces the error with a specific signature (and\n+//     specifically NOT \"unexpected end of JSON input\", which would mean the\n+//     SSE filter regressed).\n+func TestOMLXFixtureReplay_ConsumerEndToEnd(t *testing.T) {\n+\tfixtureDir := filepath.Join(\"testdata\", \"omlx-wire\")\n+\tcases := []string{\"happy_path.jsonl\", \"error_path.jsonl\"}\n+\n+\tfor _, name := range cases {\n+\t\tname := name\n+\t\tt.Run(name, func(t *testing.T) {\n+\t\t\tfix := loadOMLXFixture(t, filepath.Join(fixtureDir, name))\n+\n+\t\t\tsrv := newOMLXReplayServer(t, fix.Frames)\n+\n+\t\t\tprovider := openai.New(openai.Config{\n+\t\t\t\tBaseURL: srv.URL + \"/v1\",\n+\t\t\t\tAPIKey:  \"test\",\n+\t\t\t\tModel:   \"Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit\",\n+\t\t\t\t// Flavor \"omlx\" pins the capability table to the one used in\n+\t\t\t\t// production for vidar-omlx routing — matches the real\n+\t\t\t\t// dispatch path.\n+\t\t\t\tFlavor: \"omlx\",\n+\t\t\t})\n+\n+\t\t\tr := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: t.TempDir(), TimeoutMS: 10000})\n+\t\t\tr.LookPath = mockLookPath\n+\t\t\tr.AgentProvider = provider\n+\n+\t\t\t// A WorkDir prevents the agent from using the live worktree when\n+\t\t\t// it invokes its built-in read/write/bash tools during the loop.\n+\t\t\tresult, err := r.RunAgent(RunOptions{\n+\t\t\t\tHarness: \"agent\",\n+\t\t\t\tPrompt:  \"smoke test — omlx fixture replay\",\n+\t\t\t\tWorkDir: t.TempDir(),\n+\t\t\t})\n+\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"RunAgent must not return a plumbing error; failures are reported via result.ExitCode / result.Error\")\n+\n+\t\t\t// The regression signature is always disallowed — on BOTH paths.\n+\t\t\t// v0.3.13 would surface this even for well-formed streams when\n+\t\t\t// keep-alive comment frames were present.\n+\t\t\tif fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorMustNotContain != \"\" {\n+\t\t\t\tassert.NotContains(t, result.Error, fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorMustNotContain,\n+\t\t\t\t\t\"consumer surfaced the v0.3.13 SSE comment-frame regression signature — the sseCommentFilter in ddx-agent v0.3.14+ is missing or broken\")\n+\t\t\t}\n+\n+\t\t\tswitch fix.Meta.Expected.Status {\n+\t\t\tcase \"success\":\n+\t\t\t\tassert.Equal(t, 0, result.ExitCode, \"happy-path fixture must close cleanly; got error=%q\", result.Error)\n+\t\t\t\tassert.Empty(t, result.Error, \"happy-path must produce no error\")\n+\t\t\t\tif want := fix.Meta.Expected.ContentContains; want != \"\" {\n+\t\t\t\t\tassert.Contains(t, result.Output, want,\n+\t\t\t\t\t\t\"happy-path must assemble content from post-keep-alive data frames\")\n+\t\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tcase \"error\":\n+\t\t\t\tassert.Equal(t, 1, result.ExitCode, \"error-path fixture must report failure via ExitCode=1\")\n+\t\t\t\tassert.NotEmpty(t, result.Error, \"error-path must surface a non-empty error\")\n+\t\t\t\tif want := fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorContains; want != \"\" {\n+\t\t\t\t\tassert.Contains(t, result.Error, want,\n+\t\t\t\t\t\t\"error-path must propagate the underlying parse error\")\n+\t\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tdefault:\n+\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"fixture %s: unknown expected status %q\", name, fix.Meta.Expected.Status)\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t})\n+\t}\n+}\n+\n+// TestOMLXFixtureReplay_FixturesExist is a cheap guardrail that fails fast if\n+// the testdata/omlx-wire directory is emptied or renamed. The full replay test\n+// above would also fail in that case, but a dedicated assertion surfaces the\n+// structural problem without the noise of a streaming-test stack trace.\n+func TestOMLXFixtureReplay_FixturesExist(t *testing.T) {\n+\tfor _, name := range []string{\"happy_path.jsonl\", \"error_path.jsonl\"} {\n+\t\tp := filepath.Join(\"testdata\", \"omlx-wire\", name)\n+\t\tinfo, err := os.Stat(p)\n+\t\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"missing fixture %s\", p)\n+\t\tassert.False(t, info.IsDir(), \"%s must be a file\", p)\n+\t\tassert.NotZero(t, info.Size(), \"%s must not be empty\", p)\n+\t}\n+}\ndiff --git a/cli/internal/agent/omlx_smoke_test.go b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_smoke_test.go\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..fd43c76e\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_smoke_test.go\n@@ -0,0 +1,66 @@\n+//go:build omlx_smoke\n+\n+// Opt-in live smoke test for the ddx work → ddx-agent → vidar-omlx consumer\n+// path. Unlike the fixture-replay test in omlx_e2e_test.go (which ships in CI\n+// and uses a recorded test double), this test hits a real omlx endpoint and is\n+// intended for manual release verification against a live server. It is gated\n+// by the `omlx_smoke` build tag so the default `go test ./...` never runs it.\n+//\n+// Run:\n+//\n+//\tOMLX_SMOKE_URL=http://vidar:1235/v1\\\n+//\tOMLX_SMOKE_MODEL=Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit\\\n+//\tgo test -tags omlx_smoke -run TestOMLXLiveSmoke -v ./cli/internal/agent\n+//\n+// This covers the same happy-path shape the CI fixture does, but against a\n+// real server — useful for catching provider-side regressions (the omlx\n+// server ships a breaking change, HTTP stack differences, headers, etc.) that\n+// a recorded fixture cannot detect.\n+\n+package agent\n+\n+import (\n+\t\"os\"\n+\t\"testing\"\n+\n+\t\"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent/provider/openai\"\n+\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert\"\n+\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n+)\n+\n+func TestOMLXLiveSmoke(t *testing.T) {\n+\tbaseURL := os.Getenv(\"OMLX_SMOKE_URL\")\n+\tif baseURL == \"\" {\n+\t\tt.Skip(\"OMLX_SMOKE_URL not set — live smoke requires an omlx endpoint\")\n+\t}\n+\tmodel := os.Getenv(\"OMLX_SMOKE_MODEL\")\n+\tif model == \"\" {\n+\t\tt.Fatal(\"OMLX_SMOKE_MODEL is required when OMLX_SMOKE_URL is set\")\n+\t}\n+\n+\tprovider := openai.New(openai.Config{\n+\t\tBaseURL: baseURL,\n+\t\tAPIKey:  os.Getenv(\"OMLX_SMOKE_API_KEY\"),\n+\t\tModel:   model,\n+\t\tFlavor:  \"omlx\",\n+\t})\n+\n+\tr := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: t.TempDir(), TimeoutMS: 120000})\n+\tr.LookPath = mockLookPath\n+\tr.AgentProvider = provider\n+\n+\tresult, err := r.RunAgent(RunOptions{\n+\t\tHarness: \"agent\",\n+\t\tPrompt:  \"Reply with the single word 'ready' and nothing else.\",\n+\t\tWorkDir: t.TempDir(),\n+\t})\n+\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n+\n+\t// The regression signature is the precise error v0.3.13 surfaced when\n+\t// omlx emitted keep-alive SSE comment frames during reasoning warmup.\n+\t// Any bump that reintroduces it must be caught here before it ships.\n+\tassert.NotContains(t, result.Error, \"unexpected end of JSON input\",\n+\t\t\"live omlx produced the v0.3.13 SSE comment-frame regression signature\")\n+\tassert.Equal(t, 0, result.ExitCode, \"live omlx smoke must close cleanly; error=%q\", result.Error)\n+\tassert.NotEmpty(t, result.Output, \"live omlx must return some content\")\n+}\ndiff --git a/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/README.md b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/README.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..1f3cbdfa\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/README.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,61 @@\n+# omlx SSE Wire Fixtures\n+\n+These fixtures drive `cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go` — the consumer-side\n+end-to-end smoke test that guards `ddx work` against regressions on the\n+`ddx-agent → vidar-omlx` dispatch path. Context: bead **ddx-bbb2d177**, which\n+was filed after v0.3.14 shipped the `sseCommentFilter` fix for bead\n+**agent-f237e07b** (upstream).\n+\n+## File format\n+\n+Each fixture is a JSONL file with two record kinds:\n+\n+- First line: `{\"kind\":\"meta\", ...}` — describes the capture and the assertion\n+  shape the replay test will enforce (expected status, substrings that must or\n+  must not appear in the assembled content / error).\n+- Remaining lines: `{\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":\"<raw SSE body>\"}` — one frame per\n+  record, written verbatim (including `\\n\\n` terminators) to the test HTTP\n+  response in arrival order.\n+\n+Frames include both `: keep-alive` SSE comment frames (the exact shape that\n+triggered the v0.3.13 \"unexpected end of JSON input\" regression) and normal\n+`data: {...}` chunks.\n+\n+## When to regenerate\n+\n+Regenerate fixtures only when real omlx server behavior changes in a way the\n+existing captures no longer represent — for example, a new reasoning-phase\n+frame shape, a header shift, or a chunk-boundary change. Do NOT regenerate\n+just to refresh timestamps.\n+\n+## How to regenerate\n+\n+Use `scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh`, which wraps `ddx agent run` with\n+`AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1` (shipped in ddx-agent v0.3.14) so the full\n+stream is captured to a sidecar JSONL, then reshapes it into the fixture\n+format:\n+\n+```bash\n+scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh \\\n+  --endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1 \\\n+  --model    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit \\\n+  --prompt   \"Reply with the word ready.\" \\\n+  --name     happy-path \\\n+  --status   success \\\n+  --out      cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl\n+```\n+\n+After the script writes the fixture, hand-edit the `meta.expected` block to\n+pin the `content_contains` / `error_contains` / `finish_reason` assertions for\n+the capture you just took — the script cannot infer those from the wire\n+bytes alone.\n+\n+## Why fixtures live here instead of the upstream agent repo\n+\n+The agent repo (`DocumentDrivenDX/agent`) has its own provider-level SSE\n+tests in `provider/openai/openai_test.go`. Those guard the SDK's handling of\n+SSE framing in isolation. The fixtures in *this* directory guard the DDx\n+consumer — `ddx work` → `Runner.RunAgent` → `agentlib.Run` — against both\n+SDK regressions (a bad ddx-agent bump) and DDx-side regressions (a header or\n+request-shape change that trips omlx's parser). The two layers cover\n+different failure modes and both matter.\ndiff --git a/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/error_path.jsonl b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/error_path.jsonl\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..3dd1154f\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/error_path.jsonl\n@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@\n+{\"kind\":\"meta\",\"name\":\"error-path\",\"description\":\"omlx-style server sends role delta, a reasoning chunk, then a malformed JSON data frame. The consumer must surface the JSON parse error cleanly (NOT as 'unexpected end of JSON input', which is the v0.3.13 SSE-comment-frame regression signature). The orchestrator should observe a non-success result and record task_failed without corrupting the bead tracker.\",\"expected\":{\"status\":\"error\",\"error_must_not_contain\":\"unexpected end of JSON input\",\"error_contains\":\"invalid character\"}}\n+{\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":\": keep-alive\\n\\n\"}\n+{\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":\"data: {\\\"id\\\":\\\"chatcmpl-2\\\",\\\"model\\\":\\\"Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit\\\",\\\"choices\\\":[{\\\"index\\\":0,\\\"delta\\\":{\\\"role\\\":\\\"assistant\\\"},\\\"finish_reason\\\":null}]}\\n\\n\"}\n+{\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":\": keep-alive\\n\\n\"}\n+{\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":\"data: {\\\"id\\\":\\\"chatcmpl-2\\\",\\\"model\\\":\\\"Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit\\\",\\\"choices\\\":[{\\\"index\\\":0,\\\"delta\\\":{\\\"reasoning_content\\\":\\\"thinking...\\\"},\\\"finish_reason\\\":null}]}\\n\\n\"}\n+{\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":\"data: {this is not valid json}\\n\\n\"}\ndiff --git a/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..9eb45cbd\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl\n@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@\n+{\"kind\":\"meta\",\"name\":\"happy-path\",\"description\":\"omlx emits keep-alive SSE comment frames during Qwen3 reasoning warmup, then a role delta, reasoning_content chunks, a content delta, and a final finish_reason=stop chunk followed by [DONE]. b/scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh\n@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@\n+#!/usr/bin/env bash\n+# capture-omlx-fixture.sh — regenerate a testdata/omlx-wire fixture from a\n+# live omlx endpoint. Wraps `ddx agent run` with AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1\n+# (shipped in ddx-agent v0.3.14 — bead agent-f237e07b) and reshapes the wire\n+# dump into the fixture JSONL format the consumer-side smoke test expects.\n+#\n+# Usage:\n+#   scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh \\\n+#     --endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1 \\\n+#     --model    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit \\\n+#     --prompt   \"Reply with the word ready.\" \\\n+#     --out      cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl\n+#\n+# Required tools: ddx (v0.6.0-alpha22+), jq.\n+#\n+# What the script does:\n+#   1. Runs an isolated ddx agent invocation against the given omlx endpoint\n+#      with AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 so the whole\n+#      SSE stream is captured to a sidecar wire file.\n+#   2. Extracts every `dir==\"response\"` body chunk in arrival order and emits\n+#      one {\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":...} record per chunk.\n+#   3. Prepends a {\"kind\":\"meta\",...} record with the endpoint, model, prompt,\n+#      and a user-supplied expected outcome so the replay test knows what to\n+#      assert.\n+#\n+# Fixtures are checked in. Regenerate only when the omlx server changes wire\n+# behavior (new frame type, header shift, chunk-size change) in a way the\n+# existing fixtures no longer represent.\n+\n+set -euo pipefail\n+\n+endpoint=\"\"\n+model=\"\"\n+prompt=\"\"\n+out=\"\"\n+name=\"\"\n+status=\"success\"\n+\n+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do\n+  case \"$1\" in\n+    --endpoint) endpoint=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --model)    model=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --prompt)   prompt=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --out)      out=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --name)     name=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --status)   status=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;  # success | error\n+    -h|--help)\n+      sed -n '2,28p' \"$0\"\n+      exit 0\n+      ;;\n+    *) echo \"unknown arg: $1\" >&2; exit 2 ;;\n+  esac\n+done\n+\n+for v in endpoint model prompt out; do\n+  if [[ -z \"${!v}\" ]]; then\n+    echo \"missing required --$v\" >&2\n+    exit 2\n+  fi\n+done\n+\n+command -v jq >/dev/null || { echo \"jq is required\" >&2; exit 2; }\n+command -v ddx >/dev/null || { echo \"ddx is required\" >&2; exit 2; }\n+\n+wire_file=\"$(mktemp -t omlx-wire.XXXXXX.jsonl)\"\n+trap 'rm -f \"$wire_file\"' EXIT\n+\n+echo \"capturing wire dump from $endpoint → $wire_file\" >&2\n+\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 \\\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 \\\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_FILE=\"$wire_file\" \\\n+ddx agent run \\\n+  --harness agent \\\n+  --provider vidar-omlx \\\n+  --model \"$model\" \\\n+  --prompt-text \"$prompt\" \\\n+  --timeout 120s \\\n+  >/dev/null || {\n+    echo \"ddx agent run failed — fixture capture incomplete\" >&2\n+    echo \"wire dump (may still be useful): $wire_file\" >&2\n+    exit 1\n+  }\n+\n+[[ -z \"$name\" ]] && name=\"$(basename \"$out\" .jsonl)\"\n+\n+tmp_out=\"$(mktemp -t omlx-fixture.XXXXXX.jsonl)\"\n+trap 'rm -f \"$wire_file\" \"$tmp_out\"' EXIT\n+\n+# Meta record — expected outcome is a hint for the replay test. Authors may\n+# need to hand-edit content_contains / error_contains after inspecting the\n+# captured body.\n+jq -cn \\\n+  --arg name \"$name\" \\\n+  --arg desc \"Captured $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) from $endpoint model=$model prompt=$prompt\" \\\n+  --arg status \"$status\" \\\n+  '{kind:\"meta\", name:$name, description:$desc,\n+    expected:{status:$status, error_must_not_contain:\"unexpected end of JSON input\"}}' \\\n+  >> \"$tmp_out\"\n+\n+# Frame records — every response chunk in capture order. AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE\n+# with STREAM_FULL=1 emits one {\"dir\":\"response\",\"body\":\"…\"} record per read.\n+jq -c 'select(.dir==\"response\" and (.body|type==\"string\") and (.body|length>0))\n+       | {kind:\"frame\", body:.body}' \\\n+  \"$wire_file\" >> \"$tmp_out\"\n+\n+mv \"$tmp_out\" \"$out\"\n+echo \"wrote $(wc -l < \"$out\") records to $out\" >&2","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:910:{"acceptance":"1. cli/cmd/work_plan.go implements 'ddx work plan' as a cobra subcommand of 'ddx work'. Help text describes the relationship to 'ddx work' (preview vs execute) and 'ddx bead ready' (picker-aware vs ready-set).

2. Picker filter+sort logic extracted from cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go nextCandidate into a reusable function PreviewQueue(filters PickerFilters) []QueueEntry. ExecuteBeadWorker.nextCandidate now calls PreviewQueue + skips attempted-map members; behavior unchanged.

3. 'ddx work plan' default output: top 10 ready+eligible beads in picker order, with columns Position, Bead ID, Priority, Updated, Status, Filter-decision, Why.

4. 'ddx work plan --limit=0' returns the full queue including skip reasons for non-eligible beads.

5. 'ddx work plan --label-filter=phase:2,area:agent' filters identically to the worker's label intersection.

6. 'ddx work plan --capabilities=bead-attempt' filters identically to the worker's capability matching (when capabilities are implemented; today this is a no-op pass-through).

7. 'ddx work plan --json' emits an ordered JSON array with the same fields, suitable for piping to jq.

8. WIRED-IN: 'ddx work plan' is registered as a subcommand under 'ddx work' (or as a sibling top-level if cobra doesn't naturally support subcommands of leaf commands — verify the existing 'ddx work' structure first). Verified by 'ddx work plan --help' output AND by 'git grep "planCmd\|NewPlanCommand\|workPlanCmd" cli/cmd/' showing the registration. Worker code path imports and calls the extracted PreviewQueue function — verified by 'git grep PreviewQueue cli/internal/agent/' showing the call site.

9. Tests in cli/cmd/work_plan_test.go AND cli/internal/agent/preview_queue_test.go:
   - TestPreviewQueue_PrioritySort — fixture queue P0+P1+P2 mixed; assert P0 first, FIFO within priority
   - TestPreviewQueue_LabelFilter — fixture queue with mixed labels; assert filter intersection narrows correctly
   - TestPreviewQueue_CooldownSkip — fixture bead with execute-loop-retry-after in future; assert skip reason names the time
   - TestPreviewQueue_DepsBlocked — fixture bead with unclosed dep; assert skip reason names the dep
   - TestPreviewQueue_DeterministicAcrossRuns — call PreviewQueue twice with same inputs; assert byte-identical output (no per-Run state leaking in)
   - TestWorkPlan_CLI_TextOutput — assert default text format matches the documented columns
   - TestWorkPlan_CLI_JSONOutput — assert --json output is valid JSON parseable by jq
   - TestPicker_StillWorks_AfterRefactor — assert ExecuteBeadWorker.nextCandidate still returns the first eligible candidate identically to pre-refactor behavior

10. Quality gates:
   - cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...
   - lefthook run pre-commit

11. Conventional commit message ending [<this-bead-id>]. Stage cli/cmd/work_plan*.go + cli/internal/agent/preview_queue.go + cli/cmd/work.go (subcommand registration line) + the modified cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (only the nextCandidate extraction). DO NOT use git add -A.

12. Manual verification (operator runs after merge):
   - 'ddx work plan' shows current top-of-queue with priorities P0 first
   - 'ddx work plan --limit=0 --json | jq '.[].id'' lists every bead in determined picker order
   - Comparing 'ddx work plan' output to actual ddx work claim events should show identical bead IDs in identical order","created_at":"2026-05-03T20:07:58.299023187Z","description":"PROBLEM
Operators cannot inspect what 'ddx work' would pick without actually picking. When an unexpected bead claims (or an expected bead is skipped), the only diagnostic is the diagnostic events emitted AFTER claim attempt — by then the operator has already lost the chance to intervene. There is no read-only 'show me your decision' surface.

ROOT CAUSE
The picker logic at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1145 (ExecuteBeadWorker.nextCandidate) reads the bead store via Store.ReadyExecution (cli/internal/bead/store.go:1246) and applies filters (per-Run attempted map, label_filter intersection) before returning the first eligible candidate. The picker is invoked only INSIDE the loop — there is no standalone API to query 'what would you pick?'.

The existing 'ddx bead ready' command returns ready beads but does NOT apply the picker's per-Run attempted filter (which is non-deterministic across runs anyway), nor does it visualize the picker's tie-breaking rules (priority asc, then updated_at asc per FIFO).

Bead metadata IS deterministic. Given the bead store + stable filter inputs (label_filter, capabilities), the picker's output for the FIRST eligible bead must be 100% deterministic. There is no reason this shouldn't be inspectable interactively.

NAMING NOTE
Originally proposed as 'ddx queue plan'. Renamed to 'ddx work plan' because 'queue' is not an established concept in the CLI surface; 'work' is (the existing 'ddx work' drain command). This makes the new subcommand a natural sibling: 'ddx work' executes the drain; 'ddx work plan' previews what the drain would do.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a new subcommand at cli/cmd/work_plan.go (sibling file to cli/cmd/work.go):

  ddx work plan [--label-filter=<labels>] [--capabilities=<csv>] [--limit=<N>] [--json]

Behavior:
1. Read the bead store via the same Store.ReadyExecution path the worker uses (so filters like cooldown, superseded, execution-eligible apply identically).
2. Apply the same sort the worker applies: priority asc, then updated_at asc within priority.
3. Apply optional --label-filter and --capabilities to mirror worker filtering exactly.
4. SKIP the per-Run attempted map (it's non-deterministic across runs; not a stable picker decision).
5. Output an ordered table: Position | Bead ID | Priority | Updated | Status | Filter-decision | Why
   - 'Why' column explains each bead's pick-or-skip reason: 'next claim', 'eligible (rank N)', 'skipped: in cooldown until <RFC3339>', 'skipped: label_filter mismatch (bead labels=[a,b]; worker filter=[c])', 'skipped: capability mismatch (bead requires=[review]; worker has=[bead-attempt])', 'blocked: dep <id> open', etc.
6. Default --limit=10 (top of queue); --limit=0 = full queue.
7. Support --json for machine-readable output (operator scripts; comparison with actual claim events).

Implementation strategy: extract the picker's filter+sort logic from ExecuteBeadWorker.nextCandidate into a reusable function in cli/internal/agent (or cli/internal/bead) — call it 'PreviewQueue(filters PickerFilters) []QueueEntry'. The worker's nextCandidate then calls PreviewQueue + filters out attempted; the new 'ddx work plan' command calls PreviewQueue directly.

This factoring is itself the key insight: the picker logic SHOULD be a pure function over (bead store, filters) returning an ordered list. The worker's role is just 'call picker, claim first, execute'. Separating preview from claim gives us the dry-run + makes the picker testable in isolation.

NON-SCOPE
- Modifying picker semantics (this is read-only preview)
- Predicting downstream effects (e.g., 'after I claim X, what's next?' — out of scope; operator can re-run plan after each claim)
- Server-side picker integration (rev 5 architecture: there is no server-side picker; this command queries the bead store directly)
- Removing the per-Run attempted map (that's owned by C7 ddx-06eb05d8)
- Making 'ddx work' itself accept --plan as a flag instead (subcommand is clearer scope)

INTERSECTION
- Pairs with 'ddx try <bead-id>' (ddx-f261d2a0): operator runs 'ddx work plan' to see ordering, then 'ddx try <id>' to dispatch deterministically.
- Picker priority bug bead ddx-9d55601f shipped diagnostic events at commit 80f51574 (picker.priority_skip, picker.claim_race). 'ddx work plan' is the COMPLEMENT: events show what HAPPENED; plan shows what WOULD happen.
- C8 (ddx-848069a3) relocates routing preflight; 'ddx work plan' should NOT call routing preflight (it's a 'show me the queue' query, not 'show me what could execute given routing health').","id":"ddx-bc6d36e0","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:cli","area:agent","kind:feature","reliability","observability","wave:0"],"notes":"Shipped at commit c0f95380: PreviewQueue + ddx work plan subcommand. 13 tests added.","parent":"ddx-e34994e2","priority":0,"status":"closed","title":"cli: add 'ddx work plan' — dry-run picker preview showing exact ordering ddx work would pick","updated_at":"2026-05-03T20:32:54.490420167Z"}

========================================================

Status: AC7 already satisfied on main; no further code change needed in
this attempt.

What is done
------------
A prior attempt landed commit efbed403 ("feat(agent): add prompt_sha to
manifest + prompt-size CI fixture [ddx-bffb23ae]") which is an ancestor
of HEAD/main in this worktree. Verified with `git merge-base HEAD
efbed403` → efbed403. That commit delivers every AC7 deliverable:

1. prompt_sha in manifest.json for every attempt
   - Field defined: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:177-181
     (`PromptSHA string \`json:"prompt_sha,omitempty"\``)
   - Populated on write: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1127
     (`PromptSHA: promptSHA(promptContent)`)
   - Helper: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1467-1470
     (`promptSHA` returns hex sha256 of the rendered prompt bytes)
   - Regression test: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_artifacts_test.go
     :230-298 asserts manifest.prompt_sha is a 64-char hex digest and
     equals sha256 of the prompt.md bytes on disk.

2. Render-size CI fixture emitting word/byte counts per (variant,
   contextBudget)
   - Test: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_promptsize_test.go
     (`TestPromptSizeReport`) renders both variants
     (claude harness, agent harness) at contextBudget = "" (full) and
     "minimal", logs the table to test output, writes a tab-separated
     human report and a JSON sibling, and asserts minimal < full bytes
     per variant to catch cheap-tier regressions.
   - Determinism guard: `TestPromptSHA_DeterministicAcrossRenders`
     asserts the helper is stable across renders and differs across
     variants.

3. CI surfaces the report as an artifact
   - .github/workflows/ci.yml:201-214 runs the fixture with
     `DDX_PROMPT_SIZE_REPORT=${{ github.workspace }}/prompt-size-report
     .txt` and uploads `prompt-size-report.{txt,json,log}` as the
     `prompt-size-report` artifact.

Verification run in this worktree
---------------------------------
`cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run
'TestPromptSizeReport|TestPromptSHA|ExecuteBeadArtifacts' -count=1` →
ok (0.012s).

Why the bead was re-queued
--------------------------
The notes section on this bead reads "REVIEW:BLOCK — Diff contains only
this attempt's own execution evidence ... The shown manifest.json itself
lacks a prompt_sha field, contradicting AC7." That review fired against
a *different* attempt whose diff happened to contain only the evidence
files (manifest.json/result.json) for that attempt's own execution
bundle. The actual implementation landed on a separate commit
(efbed403) which is now on main. The recorded manifest.json the
reviewer inspected was generated by an *earlier* execution that ran
before efbed403 was merged, so it predates the field — the manifest.json
emitted by the current binary does include prompt_sha.

What a follow-up attempt would need
-----------------------------------
Nothing for AC7. If the reviewer still doubts the field is being
written, point them at the fresh manifest.json in this attempt's

rationale: Bead ddx-bffb23ae (Story 12 B3) — no_changes rationale
========================================================

Status: AC7 already satisfied on main; no further code change needed in
this attempt.

What is done
------------
A prior attempt landed commit efbed403 ("feat(agent): add prompt_sha to
manifest + prompt-size CI fixture [ddx-bffb23ae]") which is an ancestor
of HEAD/main in this worktree. Verified with `git merge-base HEAD
efbed403` → efbed403. That commit delivers every AC7 deliverable:

1. prompt_sha in manifest.json for every attempt
   - Field defined: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:177-181
     (`PromptSHA string \`json:"prompt_sha,omitempty"\``)
   - Populated on write: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1127
     (`PromptSHA: promptSHA(promptContent)`)
   - Helper: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1467-1470
     (`promptSHA` returns hex sha256 of the rendered prompt bytes)
   - Regression test: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_artifacts_test.go
     :230-298 asserts manifest.prompt_sha is a 64-char hex digest and
     equals sha256 of the prompt.md bytes on disk.

2. Render-size CI fixture emitting word/byte counts per (variant,
   contextBudget)
   - Test: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_promptsize_test.go
     (`TestPromptSizeReport`) renders both variants
     (claude harness, agent harness) at contextBudget = "" (full) and
     "minimal", logs the table to test output, writes a tab-separated
     human report and a JSON sibling, and asserts minimal < full bytes
     per variant to catch cheap-tier regressions.
   - Determinism guard: `TestPromptSHA_DeterministicAcrossRenders`
     asserts the helper is stable across renders and differs across
     variants.

3. CI surfaces the report as an artifact
   - .github/workflows/ci.yml:201-214 runs the fixture with
     `DDX_PROMPT_SIZE_REPORT=${{ github.workspace }}/prompt-size-report
     .txt` and uploads `prompt-size-report.{txt,json,log}` as the
     `prompt-size-report` artifact.

Verification run in this worktree
---------------------------------
`cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run
'TestPromptSizeReport|TestPromptSHA|ExecuteBeadArtifacts' -count=1` →
ok (0.012s).

Why the bead was re-queued
--------------------------
The notes section on this bead reads "REVIEW:BLOCK — Diff contains only
this attempt's own execution evidence ... The shown manifest.json itself
lacks a prompt_sha field, contradicting AC7." That review fired against
a *different* attempt whose diff happened to contain only the evidence
files (manifest.json/result.json) for that attempt's own execution
bundle. The actual implementation landed on a separate commit
(efbed403) which is now on main. The recorded manifest.json the
reviewer inspected was generated by an *earlier* execution that ran
before efbed403 was merged, so it predates the field — the manifest.json
emitted by the current binary does include prompt_sha.

What a follow-up attempt would need
-----------------------------------
Nothing for AC7. If the reviewer still doubts the field is being
written, point them at the fresh manifest.json in this attempt's
evidence directory (.ddx/executions/20260502T121152-24b051ff/
manifest.json) and the unit test
TestExecuteBeadArtifacts_ManifestPromptSHA in execute_bead_artifacts_
test.go which asserts prompt_sha is present and matches sha256 of
prompt.md.

rationale: status: needs_investigation
reason: |
  ddx-d7aca866 is a rollup/tracking epic for ADR-022 rev 5. Its acceptance criteria
  are not directly implementable in a single agent attempt:

    - "All 9 implementation children closed (steps 1-9 from the roadmap)"
    - "Coordination bead 10 closed (C7 conflict resolved)"
    - "FEAT-006 reflects rev 5"
    - "Acceptance bead's soak passed for ≥1 week"  ← calendar-time gate
    - "CHANGELOG entry merged"

  Closure is gated on (a) each child bead being independently executed and closed,
  and (b) a ≥1-week soak window that cannot be compressed by an agent. The epic
  itself has no code/test deliverable; it is a rollup whose status should flip to
  closed only after the last child closes and the soak window elapses.

  Current child inventory (queried via `ddx bead list --json | jq 'parent==epic'`):
    - ddx-2892f2cf  open   step 1: server ingestion endpoints (register + event + backfill)
    - ddx-b6ab9c0b  open   step 8: FEAT-006 amendment — replace rev 3 worker-contract with rev 5

  The epic description says "The 9 implementation beads + 1 coordination bead are
  children of this epic," but only 2 of the 10 are filed. Steps 2–7, step 9, and
  the coordination bead (step 10 / C7 resolution) have not been created. Filing
  those slices is a triage decision (scope, ordering, owners) that should be
  done by a human or a dedicated decomposition pass against the ADR roadmap —
  not auto-generated mid-execution loop, because the ADR-022 rev 5 roadmap
  sections name specific contracts (auth, schema, retry, ack semantics) that
  warrant an explicit triage review before bead text is committed.

  Recommended next action for the human triager:
    1. Read docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-022-worker-client-server-architecture.md
       (commit 484b9a08) "Implementation roadmap" section.
    2. Create beads for steps 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9 and the coordination bead
       (step 10 / C7), each as a child of ddx-d7aca866 via `ddx bead dep add`.
    3. Drain the resulting child queue normally; this epic will be closable
       once they all close and the soak/CHANGELOG ACs are satisfied.

  No verification_command is provided because none of {child closure, 1-week
  soak, CHANGELOG merge} can be checked by a 60s shell command from this
  worktree. The epic should remain open for human triage.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:1069:{"acceptance":"1. Default --poll-interval = 30s in all four call sites: cli/cmd/work.go:66, cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1568, cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:115, cli/internal/server/server.go:2281. 2. execute_bead_loop.go:440-441 no longer returns early when poll-interval > 0; sleeps + retries instead. Per-Run 'attempted'/'hookFailed' maps reset on no-candidate path. 3. execute_bead_loop.go:483-508 preflight rejection becomes per-bead continue, not loop exit. 4. loop.end event includes structured exit_reason field. Recognized values include 'sigterm', 'sigint', 'fatal_config', 'once_complete', 'explicit_poll_zero'; 'providers_exhausted' slot reserved for ddx-aede917d. 5. Server-managed workers report no_ready_work as transient (worker stays in 'running' state with 'idle' substate); existing 'exited' status reserved for true exits per AC #4. 6. Tests: TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue (poll-interval > 0; verify loop stays alive across N empty polls); TestLoop_PreflightRejectionContinues (one bead's preflight failure doesn't abandon siblings); TestLoop_OnceFlagStillExits (back-compat); TestLoop_ExplicitPollZeroExits (back-compat). 7. Manual verification: spawn worker via 'ddx work --harness claude'; let queue drain; verify worker stays running > 5 min after queue empty (vs current behavior of immediate exit). 8. Update FEAT-006 / FEAT-010 docs noting the new long-running default + the explicit opt-out flags.","closing_commit_sha":"c8841f9978d5dfe595c33537ac25eebda74b3e30","created_at":"2026-05-02T19:40:27.168186276Z","description":"Workers exit prematurely even when ready beads remain in the queue. Operators have to manually relaunch, defeating the point of a long-running drain.

## Root causes (from sub-agent investigation)

1. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:440-441**: when nextCandidate returns no eligible bead AND runtime.PollInterval <= 0, the loop returns immediately. Combined with the per-Run 'attempted' map (line 388, 476), once each currently-ready bead has been *attempted once* the loop exits even though those same beads (or new arrivals) are still queue-ready.

2. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:483-508**: routing-preflight rejection on a single bead exits the entire loop ('return result, nil'), abandoning all other ready beads.

3. **Default --poll-interval=0 everywhere**: cli/cmd/work.go:66, cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1568, cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:115, cli/internal/server/server.go:2281. All three recent server-spawned workers in .ddx/workers/ show poll_interval: '0s', so cause #1 fires the first empty poll.

## Fix shape (locked decisions baked in)

- Flip default --poll-interval from 0 → **30s** everywhere (CLI + server adapter). Operator can override with --once for one-shot or explicit --poll-interval=N.
- Reset per-Run 'attempted'/'hookFailed' maps when the loop falls through to 'no candidate', before sleeping. Prevents the 'attempted-once = exit' trap.
- Convert preflight early-exit (483-508) to per-bead 'continue' so one bead's routing failure doesn't abandon the rest.
- Add structured exit_reason to loop.end event; reserve 'providers_exhausted' slot for ddx-aede917d (quota-pause work).
- Server-managed workers expose 'no_ready_work' as a **transient 'running (idle)' substate**, not terminal. Worker stays alive; UI shows running (idle).
- Preserve --once and explicit --poll-interval=0 for back-compat (legacy 'drain-and-exit' semantics).

Acceptable exit conditions remain (per existing beads):
- SIGTERM / SIGINT (operator-driven)
- All providers durably out (per ddx-aede917d quota pause/resume — workers pause+probe, not exit)
- No providers configured at all (fatal config error)

Manual verification (operator runs after merge): 'ddx try ddx-50da9674' (closed fixture-repo bead) returns non-zero with the closed-bead message; 'ddx try <some-ready-bead>' actually claims and executes it.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T20:06:45Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"463338","closing_commit_sha":"ff5e64cecc17f3094f3933986966b94a50dc4f59","created_at":"2026-05-03T20:06:37.62309311Z","description":"PROBLEM
Operators have no way to dispatch a SPECIFIC bead through the production execution machinery (claim → try.Attempt → land → review). Today's surfaces:
- 'ddx work' drains by picker priority — can't force a specific bead
- 'ddx work --once' drains the top of the queue once — still picker-controlled
- 'ddx agent run --prompt' invokes the agent but doesn't claim, doesn't merge, doesn't write to bead event log

This blocks: (1) deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution, (2) the 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) which needs a way to target one fixture bead at a time, (3) operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain.

ROOT CAUSE
'ddx try' was originally bundled into refactor child C13 (ddx-387a0178) along with the file rename. C13 is sequenced AFTER C7 / C9 because the rename touches execute_bead_loop.go which those children also touch. But 'ddx try' itself is ADDITIVE — a new CLI subcommand that delegates to existing machinery — and doesn't conflict with the refactor children. Splitting 'ddx try' out lets it ship FIRST and unblocks operator-controlled execution for the entire rest of the refactor work.

Code today (relevant entry points):
- cli/cmd/work.go: 'ddx work' command implementation (calls into agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime)
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: 'ddx agent execute-loop' (the underlying loop runner)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: ExecuteBeadWorker.Run + nextCandidate
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1246 ReadyExecution + Claim

PROPOSED FIX
Add a new top-level command at cli/cmd/try.go:

  ddx try <bead-id> [--harness=<name>] [--model=<name>] [--profile=<name>]

Behavior:
1. Look up the bead by ID via the bead store; fail-fast with a clear message if not found.
2. Verify the bead is in a claimable state (status open OR stale-in_progress per existing semantics); fail-fast if status closed/cancelled OR if it has unmet dependencies.
3. Force-target this single bead. Two implementation strategies (pick simpler):
   STRATEGY A (thin wrapper): construct an ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime with --once + a custom nextCandidate that returns ONLY this bead-id, skipping the picker. Call ExecuteBeadWorker.Run.
   STRATEGY B (direct invocation): claim the bead, build the runtime config, call try.Attempt directly (or whatever the underlying single-bead execution function is today). Skip the loop.
   Recommend STRATEGY A — reuses the existing claim + executor + outcome paths; smaller diff; less risk of behavior divergence.
4. Honor --harness / --model / --profile / --opaque-passthrough exactly the way 'ddx work' does (same flag plumbing).
5. Exit with the bead's terminal status (success → 0; preserved/no_changes/blocked → non-zero with structured stderr).

NON-SCOPE
- File rename of execute_bead_loop.go (still owned by C13)
- Refactor of execute-bead surface into try/work packages (refactor epic)
- Multi-bead try (use 'ddx work' for that)
- Server-side targeting (a future enhancement; v1 is local CLI only)
- Auto-discovery of dependencies (operator must use 'ddx bead ready' first)

INTERSECTION
- C13 (ddx-387a0178) currently includes 'ADD ddx try CLI command' in its scope. After this bead lands, amend C13 to drop that line.
- 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) Path A uses 'ddx try' as the production-path executor; this bead unblocks that study.

.ddx/executions/20260506T090438-3bf70a63/prompt.md:660:+    "description": "PROBLEM
`ddx work` currently refreshes claimed-bead liveness by rewriting `.ddx/beads.jsonl` every heartbeat interval. In this repo that dirties the git worktree continuously, creates tracker-only commit noise, increases merge conflict risk on the hottest tracker file, and mixes ephemeral worker liveness with durable bead state. FEAT-006 also states the worker/server boundary has no claim lease or heartbeat, so durable tracker heartbeats conflict with the intended execution contract.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:817-832 starts a heartbeat goroutine for each claimed candidate and calls `w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID)` on every ticker interval.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:666-687 implements `Store.Heartbeat` by writing `execute-loop-heartbeat-at` and `updated_at` back into `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
- cli/internal/config/types.go:160-164 and 198-206 expose a 30s default worker heartbeat interval, making the tracker rewrite periodic during normal operation.

PROPOSED FIX
- Stop writing periodic liveness heartbeats to `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. Durable tracker mutations should remain limited to semantic transitions such as claim, unclaim, close, cooldown, and append-only evidence/events.
- Replace `Store.Heartbeat` usage in the work loop with an ephemeral liveness surface that does not dirty the tracker. Acceptable implementations include an ignored runtime lease file under `.ddx/workers/` or `.ddx/runs/`, server worker state, or another non-tracker runtime store.
- Preserve stale-claim/watchdog behavior by teaching any stale-worker logic to read the new ephemeral liveness surface or, if no reliable runtime heartbeat exists, to rely only on explicit claim metadata plus process/server lifecycle cleanup.
- Update C11/heartbeat extraction expectations if necessary so it extracts or removes the new safe liveness path rather than preserving tracker writes.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing bead claim semantics (`status=in_progress`, owner, claimed-at, claimed-pid).
- Removing append-only execution evidence or semantic bead events.
- Implementing the Axon bead backend; this bug applies to the JSONL tracker path.

DEPS
- Depends on ddx-06eb05d8 because C7 is currently changing the same drain-loop area.

INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-0b8780db (C11 heartbeat extraction) should depend on this bead so the extraction does not preserve tracker-writing heartbeat behavior.
- ddx-5cb6e6cd execution refactor should not close while periodic `.ddx/beads.jsonl` liveness writes remain in the work loop.",

  The drain loop tracks attempted and hookFailed beads via ad-hoc in-memory maps rather than a structured Guard contract, making the loop body untestable in isolation and the skip policy implicit.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413 declares attempted := make(map[string]struct{}) and hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{}). Lines 534-544 manually clear both maps between sleep cycles. Lines 591-601 implement the two-strikes pre-claim hook policy by mutating these maps inline. nextCandidate (line 1216) accepts attempted as a parameter and checks membership at line 1223. No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Define Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go: Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, reason string).
  - Implement two concrete Guards: PreClaimGuard (wraps runtime.PreClaimHook two-strikes logic) and ComplexityGuard (wraps w.ComplexityGate nil-check).
  - Delete attempted and hookFailed maps from execute_bead_loop.go:403-413. Replace nextCandidate's attempted parameter with drain.result.Results slice (already populated per-iteration): filter beads whose ID appears in Results.
  - Pre-claim hook denial: call w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, 30s, ...) instead of recording in hookFailed.
  - Add TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips and TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce to a new cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Any change to SetExecutionCooldown duration policy (controlled by caller constants).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: guard denials should skip the candidate rather than wedge the queue.
- P2: each guard owns only its own skip policy.
- P4: a denied bead stays isolated from other beads.",

.ddx/executions/20260506T080416-b75944f2/prompt.md:8:PROBLEM
  The drain loop tracks attempted and hookFailed beads via ad-hoc in-memory maps rather than a structured Guard contract, making the loop body untestable in isolation and the skip policy implicit.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413 declares attempted := make(map[string]struct{}) and hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{}). Lines 534-544 manually clear both maps between sleep cycles. Lines 591-601 implement the two-strikes pre-claim hook policy by mutating these maps inline. nextCandidate (line 1216) accepts attempted as a parameter and checks membership at line 1223. No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Define Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go: Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, reason string).
  - Implement two concrete Guards: PreClaimGuard (wraps runtime.PreClaimHook two-strikes logic) and ComplexityGuard (wraps w.ComplexityGate nil-check).
  - Delete attempted and hookFailed maps from execute_bead_loop.go:403-413. Replace nextCandidate's attempted parameter with drain.result.Results slice (already populated per-iteration): filter beads whose ID appears in Results.
  - Pre-claim hook denial: call w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, 30s, ...) instead of recording in hookFailed.
  - Add TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips and TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce to a new cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Any change to SetExecutionCooldown duration policy (controlled by caller constants).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: guard denials should skip the candidate rather than wedge the queue.
- P2: each guard owns only its own skip policy.
- P4: a denied bead stays isolated from other beads.
.ddx/executions/20260506T080416-b75944f2/prompt.md:11:1. Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go with Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, string) signature.
2. PreClaimGuard and ComplexityGuard implement Guard; loop body passes them to nextCandidate.
3. attempted and hookFailed maps deleted from cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413; no references remain.
4. nextCandidate signature no longer accepts attempted map; filters via drain.result.Results instead.
5. Pre-claim hook denial calls w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown with 30s cooldown; hookFailed logic removed.
6. TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips in cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go: two consecutive Allow calls with hook error → second returns false.
7. TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce: nil ComplexityGate → one warning, allow proceeds.
8. Existing TestPreClaimHookDivergedSkipsBead in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_preclaim_test.go still passes.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/agent/work/... green.
10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

.ddx/executions/20260506T085009-7335c519/manifest.json:12:    "description": "PROBLEM
`ddx work` currently refreshes claimed-bead liveness by rewriting `.ddx/beads.jsonl` every heartbeat interval. In this repo that dirties the git worktree continuously, creates tracker-only commit noise, increases merge conflict risk on the hottest tracker file, and mixes ephemeral worker liveness with durable bead state. FEAT-006 also states the worker/server boundary has no claim lease or heartbeat, so durable tracker heartbeats conflict with the intended execution contract.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:817-832 starts a heartbeat goroutine for each claimed candidate and calls `w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID)` on every ticker interval.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:666-687 implements `Store.Heartbeat` by writing `execute-loop-heartbeat-at` and `updated_at` back into `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
- cli/internal/config/types.go:160-164 and 198-206 expose a 30s default worker heartbeat interval, making the tracker rewrite periodic during normal operation.

PROPOSED FIX
- Stop writing periodic liveness heartbeats to `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. Durable tracker mutations should remain limited to semantic transitions such as claim, unclaim, close, cooldown, and append-only evidence/events.
- Replace `Store.Heartbeat` usage in the work loop with an ephemeral liveness surface that does not dirty the tracker. Acceptable implementations include an ignored runtime lease file under `.ddx/workers/` or `.ddx/runs/`, server worker state, or another non-tracker runtime store.
- Preserve stale-claim/watchdog behavior by teaching any stale-worker logic to read the new ephemeral liveness surface or, if no reliable runtime heartbeat exists, to rely only on explicit claim metadata plus process/server lifecycle cleanup.
- Update C11/heartbeat extraction expectations if necessary so it extracts or removes the new safe liveness path rather than preserving tracker writes.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing bead claim semantics (`status=in_progress`, owner, claimed-at, claimed-pid).
- Removing append-only execution evidence or semantic bead events.
- Implementing the Axon bead backend; this bug applies to the JSONL tracker path.

DEPS
- Depends on ddx-06eb05d8 because C7 is currently changing the same drain-loop area.

INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-0b8780db (C11 heartbeat extraction) should depend on this bead so the extraction does not preserve tracker-writing heartbeat behavior.
- ddx-5cb6e6cd execution refactor should not close while periodic `.ddx/beads.jsonl` liveness writes remain in the work loop.",

reason: |
  ddx-d7aca866 is a rollup/tracking epic for ADR-022 rev 5. Its acceptance criteria
  are not directly implementable in a single agent attempt:

    - "All 9 implementation children closed (steps 1-9 from the roadmap)"
    - "Coordination bead 10 closed (C7 conflict resolved)"
    - "FEAT-006 reflects rev 5"
    - "Acceptance bead's soak passed for ≥1 week"  ← calendar-time gate
    - "CHANGELOG entry merged"

  Closure is gated on (a) each child bead being independently executed and closed,
  and (b) a ≥1-week soak window that cannot be compressed by an agent. The epic
  itself has no code/test deliverable; it is a rollup whose status should flip to
  closed only after the last child closes and the soak window elapses.

  Current child inventory (queried via `ddx bead list --json | jq 'parent==epic'`):
    - ddx-2892f2cf  open   step 1: server ingestion endpoints (register + event + backfill)
    - ddx-b6ab9c0b  open   step 8: FEAT-006 amendment — replace rev 3 worker-contract with rev 5

  The epic description says "The 9 implementation beads + 1 coordination bead are
  children of this epic," but only 2 of the 10 are filed. Steps 2–7, step 9, and
  the coordination bead (step 10 / C7 resolution) have not been created. Filing
  those slices is a triage decision (scope, ordering, owners) that should be
  done by a human or a dedicated decomposition pass against the ADR roadmap —
  not auto-generated mid-execution loop, because the ADR-022 rev 5 roadmap
  sections name specific contracts (auth, schema, retry, ack semantics) that
  warrant an explicit triage review before bead text is committed.

  Recommended next action for the human triager:
    1. Read docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-022-worker-client-server-architecture.md
       (commit 484b9a08) "Implementation roadmap" section.
    2. Create beads for steps 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9 and the coordination bead
       (step 10 / C7), each as a child of ddx-d7aca866 via `ddx bead dep add`.
    3. Drain the resulting child queue normally; this epic will be closable
       once they all close and the soak/CHANGELOG ACs are satisfied.

  No verification_command is provided because none of {child closure, 1-week
  soak, CHANGELOG merge} can be checked by a 60s shell command from this
  worktree. The epic should remain open for human triage.
