.ddx/executions/20260505T141415-de904250/manifest.json:12:    "description": "The ddx try / ddx work execution machinery has 9 fail-prone layers (routing pre-flight, cooldown classification, claim CAS, worktree spawn, subprocess lifecycle, outcome adjudication, retry policy, land coordination, review). Today most layers fail-CLOSED — when a layer's specific check rejects, the layer wedges the entire pipeline. The auto-routing rejection bug fixed at commit 3b4f5d58 demonstrated this: one preflight check went strict and the queue locked up for hours.

The 7 principles below capture what each layer should do instead. They are documentation, not code — referenced by every refactor child's AC and by future bead-authoring guidance.

PRINCIPLE STATEMENTS

P1: FAIL-OPEN at every machinery layer. When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event — it does not wedge the pipeline. The auto-routing fix (workers.go:803, commit 3b4f5d58) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists.

P2: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY per layer. Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (fizeau owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (picker owns); etc. Cross-layer concerns are the smell.

P3: OBSERVABLE DEGRADATION. Every fail-open emits a structured event surfaced in the workers panel. Operators see "preflight skipped (no operator pin)" instead of silent acceptance OR endless retry loop.

P4: BOUNDED BLAST RADIUS. A failure on bead X must not affect bead Y. Stay-alive fix at commit 41cb762e established this for preflight rejections (per-bead continue, not loop exit). Extend to all layers.

P5: OPERATOR-VISIBLE STATE. Worker reports current state (idle, claiming, executing, reviewing, blocked-on-X) at all times. No '8 hours running, 0 attempts' mystery state. ADR-022 rev 5 §Probe + freshness state model defines the worker side; UI workers panel surfaces it.

P6: AUTO-RETRY ONLY FOR TRANSIENT CLASSES. Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race → no cooldown, return to ready. Existing code: shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1545 already respects Disrupted (commit 47d8054e).

P7: BEAD = PROMPT. A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation. Investigation done, file:line citations included, concrete test names specified, explicit non-scope marked. If a sub-agent succeeds where the bead's auto-prompt failed, the BEAD failed (not the executor). Bead-authoring template enforces this; bead-quality audit (forthcoming bead) retrofits existing beads.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Code changes (this bead is doc-only)
- Per-layer reliability bead-quality fixes (separate refactor children)

INTERSECTIONS
- Each refactor child (ddx-c8f79963 C5, ddx-06eb05d8 C7, ddx-9228a484 C9, ddx-848069a3 C8, ddx-b669bb9f C6, ddx-c670ef0a C12) gets an AC line citing the principle(s) it enforces
- Bead-quality audit applies P7 to ~20 existing open beads

EVIDENCE
- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and supports P7 / bead-quality.",

2. README sections of each refactor child (C4, C5, C6, C7, C8, C9, C11, C12, C13) cite the principle(s) the child enforces. Cross-link in those bead descriptions added via ddx bead update.
3. CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md updated with one-line pointers: 'See docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md for the 7 reliability principles applied to ddx try / ddx work execution.'
4. cd cli && go test ./... still green (no code change; sanity check that nothing broke).

.ddx/executions/20260505T141415-de904250/prompt.md:8:The ddx try / ddx work execution machinery has 9 fail-prone layers (routing pre-flight, cooldown classification, claim CAS, worktree spawn, subprocess lifecycle, outcome adjudication, retry policy, land coordination, review). Today most layers fail-CLOSED — when a layer's specific check rejects, the layer wedges the entire pipeline. The auto-routing rejection bug fixed at commit 3b4f5d58 demonstrated this: one preflight check went strict and the queue locked up for hours.

The 7 principles below capture what each layer should do instead. They are documentation, not code — referenced by every refactor child's AC and by future bead-authoring guidance.

PRINCIPLE STATEMENTS

P1: FAIL-OPEN at every machinery layer. When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event — it does not wedge the pipeline. The auto-routing fix (workers.go:803, commit 3b4f5d58) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists.

P2: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY per layer. Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (fizeau owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (picker owns); etc. Cross-layer concerns are the smell.

P3: OBSERVABLE DEGRADATION. Every fail-open emits a structured event surfaced in the workers panel. Operators see "preflight skipped (no operator pin)" instead of silent acceptance OR endless retry loop.

P4: BOUNDED BLAST RADIUS. A failure on bead X must not affect bead Y. Stay-alive fix at commit 41cb762e established this for preflight rejections (per-bead continue, not loop exit). Extend to all layers.

P5: OPERATOR-VISIBLE STATE. Worker reports current state (idle, claiming, executing, reviewing, blocked-on-X) at all times. No '8 hours running, 0 attempts' mystery state. ADR-022 rev 5 §Probe + freshness state model defines the worker side; UI workers panel surfaces it.

P6: AUTO-RETRY ONLY FOR TRANSIENT CLASSES. Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race → no cooldown, return to ready. Existing code: shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1545 already respects Disrupted (commit 47d8054e).

P7: BEAD = PROMPT. A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation. Investigation done, file:line citations included, concrete test names specified, explicit non-scope marked. If a sub-agent succeeds where the bead's auto-prompt failed, the BEAD failed (not the executor). Bead-authoring template enforces this; bead-quality audit (forthcoming bead) retrofits existing beads.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Code changes (this bead is doc-only)
- Per-layer reliability bead-quality fixes (separate refactor children)

INTERSECTIONS
- Each refactor child (ddx-c8f79963 C5, ddx-06eb05d8 C7, ddx-9228a484 C9, ddx-848069a3 C8, ddx-b669bb9f C6, ddx-c670ef0a C12) gets an AC line citing the principle(s) it enforces
- Bead-quality audit applies P7 to ~20 existing open beads

EVIDENCE
- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and supports P7 / bead-quality.

  The conflict-recovery state machine (auto-merge + focused-resolve + close-or-park) is partially extracted but the description cites only the pre-extraction loop lines, leaving the executor without a clear picture of the current API boundary.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go:118 defines RunConflictRecovery(ctx, ConflictRecoveryInput) ConflictRecoveryOutput — the C4 extraction already landed. The bead description still refers to the old inline branch. The caller site is cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 (loop branches on ShouldAttemptConflictRecovery, then calls RunConflictRecovery). The try.Attempt API (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:67, func Attempt(ctx, store, beadID, AttemptOpts) (Outcome, error)) does not yet call RunConflictRecovery; conflict recovery remains loop-owned.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Migrate RunConflictRecovery invocation from execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 into try.Attempt (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go) so Attempt's return Outcome already encodes conflict-recovery disposition.
  - Add try/conflict_recovery.go: thin adapter from ConflictRecoveryOutput to Outcome (Disposition=Merged → OutcomeSuccess, Park/NeedsHuman → OutcomePark).
  - Loop caller (execute_bead_loop.go:847) delegates to try.Attempt instead of calling RunConflictRecovery directly; removes the inline branch.
  - Add TestAttempt_ConflictRecovered_ReturnsMergedOutcome and TestAttempt_ConflictUnresolvable_ReturnsPark to cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Deleting the attempted/hookFailed maps (C7, ddx-06eb05d8).
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: fail-open at the layer boundary when conflict recovery is not applicable or cannot complete cleanly.
- P2: conflict recovery owns only its own conditions; the loop should delegate rather than infer recovery policy.
- P3: the worker panel should surface conflict-recovery transitions and any degraded path.
- P4: a bad bead X must not spill into bead Y; conflict recovery stays per-bead.",

.ddx/executions/20260505T074601-9c0810e4/prompt.md:77:+    "description": "The ddx try / ddx work execution machinery has 9 fail-prone layers (routing pre-flight, cooldown classification, claim CAS, worktree spawn, subprocess lifecycle, outcome adjudication, retry policy, land coordination, review). Today most layers fail-CLOSED — when a layer's specific check rejects, the layer wedges the entire pipeline. The auto-routing rejection bug fixed at commit 3b4f5d58 demonstrated this: one preflight check went strict and the queue locked up for hours.

The 7 principles below capture what each layer should do instead. They are documentation, not code — referenced by every refactor child's AC and by future bead-authoring guidance.

PRINCIPLE STATEMENTS

P1: FAIL-OPEN at every machinery layer. When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event — it does not wedge the pipeline. The auto-routing fix (workers.go:803, commit 3b4f5d58) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists.

P2: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY per layer. Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (fizeau owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (picker owns); etc. Cross-layer concerns are the smell.

.ddx/executions/20260505T074136-36b65cad/manifest.json:12:    "description": "The ddx try / ddx work execution machinery has 9 fail-prone layers (routing pre-flight, cooldown classification, claim CAS, worktree spawn, subprocess lifecycle, outcome adjudication, retry policy, land coordination, review). Today most layers fail-CLOSED — when a layer's specific check rejects, the layer wedges the entire pipeline. The auto-routing rejection bug fixed at commit 3b4f5d58 demonstrated this: one preflight check went strict and the queue locked up for hours.

The 7 principles below capture what each layer should do instead. They are documentation, not code — referenced by every refactor child's AC and by future bead-authoring guidance.

PRINCIPLE STATEMENTS

P1: FAIL-OPEN at every machinery layer. When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event — it does not wedge the pipeline. The auto-routing fix (workers.go:803, commit 3b4f5d58) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists.

P2: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY per layer. Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (fizeau owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (picker owns); etc. Cross-layer concerns are the smell.

P3: OBSERVABLE DEGRADATION. Every fail-open emits a structured event surfaced in the workers panel. Operators see "preflight skipped (no operator pin)" instead of silent acceptance OR endless retry loop.

P4: BOUNDED BLAST RADIUS. A failure on bead X must not affect bead Y. Stay-alive fix at commit 41cb762e established this for preflight rejections (per-bead continue, not loop exit). Extend to all layers.

P5: OPERATOR-VISIBLE STATE. Worker reports current state (idle, claiming, executing, reviewing, blocked-on-X) at all times. No '8 hours running, 0 attempts' mystery state. ADR-022 rev 5 §Probe + freshness state model defines the worker side; UI workers panel surfaces it.

P6: AUTO-RETRY ONLY FOR TRANSIENT CLASSES. Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race → no cooldown, return to ready. Existing code: shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1545 already respects Disrupted (commit 47d8054e).

P7: BEAD = PROMPT. A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation. Investigation done, file:line citations included, concrete test names specified, explicit non-scope marked. If a sub-agent succeeds where the bead's auto-prompt failed, the BEAD failed (not the executor). Bead-authoring template enforces this; bead-quality audit (forthcoming bead) retrofits existing beads.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Code changes (this bead is doc-only)

.ddx/executions/20260505T040715-47bb881d/manifest.json:12:    "description": "PROBLEM
  The conflict-recovery state machine (auto-merge + focused-resolve + close-or-park) is partially extracted but the description cites only the pre-extraction loop lines, leaving the executor without a clear picture of the current API boundary.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go:118 defines RunConflictRecovery(ctx, ConflictRecoveryInput) ConflictRecoveryOutput — the C4 extraction already landed. The bead description still refers to the old inline branch. The caller site is cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 (loop branches on ShouldAttemptConflictRecovery, then calls RunConflictRecovery). The try.Attempt API (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:67, func Attempt(ctx, store, beadID, AttemptOpts) (Outcome, error)) does not yet call RunConflictRecovery; conflict recovery remains loop-owned.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Migrate RunConflictRecovery invocation from execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 into try.Attempt (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go) so Attempt's return Outcome already encodes conflict-recovery disposition.
  - Add try/conflict_recovery.go: thin adapter from ConflictRecoveryOutput to Outcome (Disposition=Merged → OutcomeSuccess, Park/NeedsHuman → OutcomePark).
  - Loop caller (execute_bead_loop.go:847) delegates to try.Attempt instead of calling RunConflictRecovery directly; removes the inline branch.
  - Add TestAttempt_ConflictRecovered_ReturnsMergedOutcome and TestAttempt_ConflictUnresolvable_ReturnsPark to cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Deleting the attempted/hookFailed maps (C7, ddx-06eb05d8).
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).",

rationale: status: needs_investigation
reason: |
  ddx-d7aca866 is a rollup/tracking epic for ADR-022 rev 5. Its acceptance criteria
  are not directly implementable in a single agent attempt:

    - "All 9 implementation children closed (steps 1-9 from the roadmap)"
    - "Coordination bead 10 closed (C7 conflict resolved)"
    - "FEAT-006 reflects rev 5"
    - "Acceptance bead's soak passed for ≥1 week"  ← calendar-time gate
    - "CHANGELOG entry merged"

  Closure is gated on (a) each child bead being independently executed and closed,
  and (b) a ≥1-week soak window that cannot be compressed by an agent. The epic
  itself has no code/test deliverable; it is a rollup whose status should flip to
  closed only after the last child closes and the soak window elapses.

  Current child inventory (queried via `ddx bead list --json | jq 'parent==epic'`):
    - ddx-2892f2cf  open   step 1: server ingestion endpoints (register + event + backfill)
    - ddx-b6ab9c0b  open   step 8: FEAT-006 amendment — replace rev 3 worker-contract with rev 5

  The epic description says "The 9 implementation beads + 1 coordination bead are
  children of this epic," but only 2 of the 10 are filed. Steps 2–7, step 9, and
  the coordination bead (step 10 / C7 resolution) have not been created. Filing
  those slices is a triage decision (scope, ordering, owners) that should be
  done by a human or a dedicated decomposition pass against the ADR roadmap —
  not auto-generated mid-execution loop, because the ADR-022 rev 5 roadmap
  sections name specific contracts (auth, schema, retry, ack semantics) that
  warrant an explicit triage review before bead text is committed.

  Recommended next action for the human triager:
    1. Read docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-022-worker-client-server-architecture.md
       (commit 484b9a08) "Implementation roadmap" section.
    2. Create beads for steps 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9 and the coordination bead
       (step 10 / C7), each as a child of ddx-d7aca866 via `ddx bead dep add`.
    3. Drain the resulting child queue normally; this epic will be closable
       once they all close and the soak/CHANGELOG ACs are satisfied.

  No verification_command is provided because none of {child closure, 1-week
  soak, CHANGELOG merge} can be checked by a 60s shell command from this
  worktree. The epic should remain open for human triage.

.ddx/executions/20260504T222006-82844da2/manifest.json:12:    "description": "The ddx try / ddx work execution machinery has 9 fail-prone layers (routing pre-flight, cooldown classification, claim CAS, worktree spawn, subprocess lifecycle, outcome adjudication, retry policy, land coordination, review). Today most layers fail-CLOSED — when a layer's specific check rejects, the layer wedges the entire pipeline. The auto-routing rejection bug fixed at commit 3b4f5d58 demonstrated this: one preflight check went strict and the queue locked up for hours.

The 7 principles below capture what each layer should do instead. They are documentation, not code — referenced by every refactor child's AC and by future bead-authoring guidance.

PRINCIPLE STATEMENTS

P1: FAIL-OPEN at every machinery layer. When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event — it does not wedge the pipeline. The auto-routing fix (workers.go:803, commit 3b4f5d58) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists.

P2: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY per layer. Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (fizeau owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (picker owns); etc. Cross-layer concerns are the smell.

P3: OBSERVABLE DEGRADATION. Every fail-open emits a structured event surfaced in the workers panel. Operators see "preflight skipped (no operator pin)" instead of silent acceptance OR endless retry loop.

P4: BOUNDED BLAST RADIUS. A failure on bead X must not affect bead Y. Stay-alive fix at commit 41cb762e established this for preflight rejections (per-bead continue, not loop exit). Extend to all layers.

P5: OPERATOR-VISIBLE STATE. Worker reports current state (idle, claiming, executing, reviewing, blocked-on-X) at all times. No '8 hours running, 0 attempts' mystery state. ADR-022 rev 5 §Probe + freshness state model defines the worker side; UI workers panel surfaces it.

P6: AUTO-RETRY ONLY FOR TRANSIENT CLASSES. Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race → no cooldown, return to ready. Existing code: shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1545 already respects Disrupted (commit 47d8054e).

P7: BEAD = PROMPT. A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation. Investigation done, file:line citations included, concrete test names specified, explicit non-scope marked. If a sub-agent succeeds where the bead's auto-prompt failed, the BEAD failed (not the executor). Bead-authoring template enforces this; bead-quality audit (forthcoming bead) retrofits existing beads.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Code changes (this bead is doc-only)
- Per-layer reliability bead-quality fixes (separate refactor children)

INTERSECTIONS
- Each refactor child (ddx-c8f79963 C5, ddx-06eb05d8 C7, ddx-9228a484 C9, ddx-848069a3 C8, ddx-b669bb9f C6, ddx-c670ef0a C12) gets an AC line citing the principle(s) it enforces
- Bead-quality audit applies P7 to ~20 existing open beads",

4. cd cli && go test ./... still green (no code change; sanity check that nothing broke).
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
6. Conventional commit ending [<this-bead-id>].",

reason: |
  ddx-d7aca866 is a rollup/tracking epic for ADR-022 rev 5. Its acceptance criteria
  are not directly implementable in a single agent attempt:

    - "All 9 implementation children closed (steps 1-9 from the roadmap)"
    - "Coordination bead 10 closed (C7 conflict resolved)"
    - "FEAT-006 reflects rev 5"
    - "Acceptance bead's soak passed for ≥1 week"  ← calendar-time gate
    - "CHANGELOG entry merged"

  Closure is gated on (a) each child bead being independently executed and closed,
  and (b) a ≥1-week soak window that cannot be compressed by an agent. The epic
  itself has no code/test deliverable; it is a rollup whose status should flip to
  closed only after the last child closes and the soak window elapses.

  Current child inventory (queried via `ddx bead list --json | jq 'parent==epic'`):
    - ddx-2892f2cf  open   step 1: server ingestion endpoints (register + event + backfill)
    - ddx-b6ab9c0b  open   step 8: FEAT-006 amendment — replace rev 3 worker-contract with rev 5

  The epic description says "The 9 implementation beads + 1 coordination bead are
  children of this epic," but only 2 of the 10 are filed. Steps 2–7, step 9, and
  the coordination bead (step 10 / C7 resolution) have not been created. Filing
  those slices is a triage decision (scope, ordering, owners) that should be
  done by a human or a dedicated decomposition pass against the ADR roadmap —
  not auto-generated mid-execution loop, because the ADR-022 rev 5 roadmap
  sections name specific contracts (auth, schema, retry, ack semantics) that
  warrant an explicit triage review before bead text is committed.

  Recommended next action for the human triager:
    1. Read docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-022-worker-client-server-architecture.md
       (commit 484b9a08) "Implementation roadmap" section.
    2. Create beads for steps 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9 and the coordination bead
       (step 10 / C7), each as a child of ddx-d7aca866 via `ddx bead dep add`.
    3. Drain the resulting child queue normally; this epic will be closable
       once they all close and the soak/CHANGELOG ACs are satisfied.

  No verification_command is provided because none of {child closure, 1-week
  soak, CHANGELOG merge} can be checked by a 60s shell command from this
  worktree. The epic should remain open for human triage.

.ddx/executions/20260503T200645-f1713f26/prompt.md:5:PROBLEM
Operators have no way to dispatch a SPECIFIC bead through the production execution machinery (claim → try.Attempt → land → review). Today's surfaces:
- 'ddx work' drains by picker priority — can't force a specific bead
- 'ddx work --once' drains the top of the queue once — still picker-controlled
- 'ddx agent run --prompt' invokes the agent but doesn't claim, doesn't merge, doesn't write to bead event log

This blocks: (1) deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution, (2) the 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) which needs a way to target one fixture bead at a time, (3) operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain.

ROOT CAUSE
'ddx try' was originally bundled into refactor child C13 (ddx-387a0178) along with the file rename. C13 is sequenced AFTER C7 / C9 because the rename touches execute_bead_loop.go which those children also touch. But 'ddx try' itself is ADDITIVE — a new CLI subcommand that delegates to existing machinery — and doesn't conflict with the refactor children. Splitting 'ddx try' out lets it ship FIRST and unblocks operator-controlled execution for the entire rest of the refactor work.

Code today (relevant entry points):
- cli/cmd/work.go: 'ddx work' command implementation (calls into agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime)
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: 'ddx agent execute-loop' (the underlying loop runner)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: ExecuteBeadWorker.Run + nextCandidate
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1246 ReadyExecution + Claim

PROPOSED FIX
Add a new top-level command at cli/cmd/try.go:

  ddx try <bead-id> [--harness=<name>] [--model=<name>] [--profile=<name>]

Behavior:
1. Look up the bead by ID via the bead store; fail-fast with a clear message if not found.
2. Verify the bead is in a claimable state (status open OR stale-in_progress per existing semantics); fail-fast if status closed/cancelled OR if it has unmet dependencies.
3. Force-target this single bead. Two implementation strategies (pick simpler):
   STRATEGY A (thin wrapper): construct an ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime with --once + a custom nextCandidate that returns ONLY this bead-id, skipping the picker. Call ExecuteBeadWorker.Run.
   STRATEGY B (direct invocation): claim the bead, build the runtime config, call try.Attempt directly (or whatever the underlying single-bead execution function is today). Skip the loop.
   Recommend STRATEGY A — reuses the existing claim + executor + outcome paths; smaller diff; less risk of behavior divergence.
4. Honor --harness / --model / --profile / --opaque-passthrough exactly the way 'ddx work' does (same flag plumbing).
5. Exit with the bead's terminal status (success → 0; preserved/no_changes/blocked → non-zero with structured stderr).

NON-SCOPE
- File rename of execute_bead_loop.go (still owned by C13)
- Refactor of execute-bead surface into try/work packages (refactor epic)
- Multi-bead try (use 'ddx work' for that)
- Server-side targeting (a future enhancement; v1 is local CLI only)
- Auto-discovery of dependencies (operator must use 'ddx bead ready' first)

INTERSECTION
- C13 (ddx-387a0178) currently includes 'ADD ddx try CLI command' in its scope. After this bead lands, amend C13 to drop that line.
- 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) Path A uses 'ddx try' as the production-path executor; this bead unblocks that study.
- Refactor children Wave 2 dispatch will use 'ddx try' (codex harness) to deterministically dispatch each child in dep order.

        cli/internal/agent/cancel_poll.go (CancelPollInterval = 10s,
        startCancelPoller); wired in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go.

        "operator_cancel"; cancel-honored:true is set:
        cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go (OperatorCancelReason,
        ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview); poll loop in
        cli/internal/agent/cancel_poll.go writes cancel-honored via
        bead.Store.HonorCancel.

  AC4 — Idempotent (second cancel is silent no-op once cancel-honored is set):
        cli/internal/bead/store.go (RequestCancel short-circuits when
        cancel-honored is already true).

        TestWorker_HonorsCancelMidAttempt, TestCancelLatency_UnderSLA:
        cli/internal/server/server_cancel_test.go:14;
        cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_cancel_test.go:99 and :166.

  AC6 — go test green; lefthook pre-commit passes (verification command
        below re-runs the cancel-related tests).

        uses a real *bead.Store + RequestCancel and a blocking runner that
        only returns when its dispatch context is cancelled — proving the
        cancel travels the live signal path, not a stub:
        cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_cancel_test.go:237.

.ddx/executions/20260503T015741-138c36ea/manifest.json:13:    "description": "OBSERVED
Two ddx workers (one server-managed, one --local --harness=claude) repeatedly claim P2 beads while four ready P0 beads sit unclaimed at the top of the queue.

REPRO (2026-05-02)
- ddx bead ready returns (top of list):
  - ddx-dc157075 P0
  - ddx-a946c744 P0 (triaged but child ddx-aee651ec P0 ready)
  - ddx-aee651ec P0
  - ddx-29058e2a P0
- Workers claim:
  - ddx-6d132f62 P2 (Story 16)
  - ddx-a7e7a1a1 P2 (Story 15-3)
  - ddx-297f840c P2 (Story 6 frontend) — earlier worker
- The 4 P0 beads have NO blocker:
  - extra.execute-loop-retry-after: null
  - extra.execution-eligible: null (defaults to true)
  - extra.superseded-by: null
  - all deps closed
- They appear in ddx bead ready AND should appear in Store.ReadyExecution() with priority=0 sorted first.

CODE PATH (per investigation)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1145 nextCandidate → calls Store.ReadyExecution()
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1246 ReadyExecution → readyFiltered(true)
- store.go:1357 calls sortBeadsForQueue(ready) which sorts by priority asc (0 first; verified at store.go:1462, 1475)
- Loop returns first candidate not in attempted map (loop.go:1150-1158)

By design this MUST return P0 first. Yet workers are getting P2. Hypotheses:
1. The `attempted` map persists across what should be fresh worker runs (worker process lifecycle bug — possibly related to ddx-dc157075).
2. A label filter is silently set on the worker spec (the server-managed worker had empty harness/model — possibly empty label filter is treated as a regex that excludes some labels).
3. Two workers race on Claim() and the loser falls through, retrying nextCandidate which now sees the bead in attempted and skips to lower-priority.
4. The server-managed worker uses a different picker path than ExecuteBeadWorker.nextCandidate (e.g., workers.go has its own selection that ignores priority).
5. The P0 beads have a label/area excluded by some default filter the operator doesn't see.

INVESTIGATION NEEDED
- Trace each currently-running worker: print its full spec (harness, model, labelFilter, oncce, pollInterval).
- Print the result of ReadyExecution() on demand (add a `ddx server workers next-candidate <worker-id>` debug command, or run from REPL).
- Add structured event when a worker passes over a P0 to claim a P2 — log the reason (in attempted, label-filtered, claim race, etc.).
- Inspect cli/internal/server/workers.go for any divergence from execute_bead_loop.go's nextCandidate.

LIKELY RELATED
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive fix) — workers exiting prematurely and the persisted `attempted` map across restarts could explain skips.
- ddx-29058e2a (server flag-drop) — if the spec is corrupted en route, label filter could be set incorrectly.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Changing the priority sort order itself.
- Adding new picker dimensions (story affinity, area concurrency caps).
- Refactoring the picker into a service (separate concern).",
.ddx/executions/20260503T200645-f1713f26/manifest.json:13:    "description": "PROBLEM
Operators have no way to dispatch a SPECIFIC bead through the production execution machinery (claim → try.Attempt → land → review). Today's surfaces:
- 'ddx work' drains by picker priority — can't force a specific bead
- 'ddx work --once' drains the top of the queue once — still picker-controlled
- 'ddx agent run --prompt' invokes the agent but doesn't claim, doesn't merge, doesn't write to bead event log

This blocks: (1) deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution, (2) the 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) which needs a way to target one fixture bead at a time, (3) operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain.

ROOT CAUSE
'ddx try' was originally bundled into refactor child C13 (ddx-387a0178) along with the file rename. C13 is sequenced AFTER C7 / C9 because the rename touches execute_bead_loop.go which those children also touch. But 'ddx try' itself is ADDITIVE — a new CLI subcommand that delegates to existing machinery — and doesn't conflict with the refactor children. Splitting 'ddx try' out lets it ship FIRST and unblocks operator-controlled execution for the entire rest of the refactor work.

Code today (relevant entry points):
- cli/cmd/work.go: 'ddx work' command implementation (calls into agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime)
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: 'ddx agent execute-loop' (the underlying loop runner)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: ExecuteBeadWorker.Run + nextCandidate
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1246 ReadyExecution + Claim

PROPOSED FIX
Add a new top-level command at cli/cmd/try.go:

  ddx try <bead-id> [--harness=<name>] [--model=<name>] [--profile=<name>]

Behavior:
1. Look up the bead by ID via the bead store; fail-fast with a clear message if not found.
2. Verify the bead is in a claimable state (status open OR stale-in_progress per existing semantics); fail-fast if status closed/cancelled OR if it has unmet dependencies.
3. Force-target this single bead. Two implementation strategies (pick simpler):
   STRATEGY A (thin wrapper): construct an ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime with --once + a custom nextCandidate that returns ONLY this bead-id, skipping the picker. Call ExecuteBeadWorker.Run.
   STRATEGY B (direct invocation): claim the bead, build the runtime config, call try.Attempt directly (or whatever the underlying single-bead execution function is today). Skip the loop.
   Recommend STRATEGY A — reuses the existing claim + executor + outcome paths; smaller diff; less risk of behavior divergence.
4. Honor --harness / --model / --profile / --opaque-passthrough exactly the way 'ddx work' does (same flag plumbing).
5. Exit with the bead's terminal status (success → 0; preserved/no_changes/blocked → non-zero with structured stderr).

NON-SCOPE
- File rename of execute_bead_loop.go (still owned by C13)
- Refactor of execute-bead surface into try/work packages (refactor epic)
- Multi-bead try (use 'ddx work' for that)
- Server-side targeting (a future enhancement; v1 is local CLI only)
- Auto-discovery of dependencies (operator must use 'ddx bead ready' first)

INTERSECTION
- C13 (ddx-387a0178) currently includes 'ADD ddx try CLI command' in its scope. After this bead lands, amend C13 to drop that line.
- 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) Path A uses 'ddx try' as the production-path executor; this bead unblocks that study.
- Refactor children Wave 2 dispatch will use 'ddx try' (codex harness) to deterministically dispatch each child in dep order.",

.ddx/executions/20260502T204356-13980032/manifest.json:13:    "description": "Workers exit prematurely even when ready beads remain in the queue. Operators have to manually relaunch, defeating the point of a long-running drain.

## Root causes (from sub-agent investigation)

1. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:440-441**: when nextCandidate returns no eligible bead AND runtime.PollInterval <= 0, the loop returns immediately. Combined with the per-Run 'attempted' map (line 388, 476), once each currently-ready bead has been *attempted once* the loop exits even though those same beads (or new arrivals) are still queue-ready.

2. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:483-508**: routing-preflight rejection on a single bead exits the entire loop ('return result, nil'), abandoning all other ready beads.

3. **Default --poll-interval=0 everywhere**: cli/cmd/work.go:66, cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1568, cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:115, cli/internal/server/server.go:2281. All three recent server-spawned workers in .ddx/workers/ show poll_interval: '0s', so cause #1 fires the first empty poll.

## Fix shape (locked decisions baked in)

- Flip default --poll-interval from 0 → **30s** everywhere (CLI + server adapter). Operator can override with --once for one-shot or explicit --poll-interval=N.
- Reset per-Run 'attempted'/'hookFailed' maps when the loop falls through to 'no candidate', before sleeping. Prevents the 'attempted-once = exit' trap.
- Convert preflight early-exit (483-508) to per-bead 'continue' so one bead's routing failure doesn't abandon the rest.
- Add structured exit_reason to loop.end event; reserve 'providers_exhausted' slot for ddx-aede917d (quota-pause work).
- Server-managed workers expose 'no_ready_work' as a **transient 'running (idle)' substate**, not terminal. Worker stays alive; UI shows running (idle).
- Preserve --once and explicit --poll-interval=0 for back-compat (legacy 'drain-and-exit' semantics).

Acceptable exit conditions remain (per existing beads):
- SIGTERM / SIGINT (operator-driven)
- All providers durably out (per ddx-aede917d quota pause/resume — workers pause+probe, not exit)
- No providers configured at all (fatal config error)
- --once explicitly requested
- --poll-interval=0 explicitly requested",

Per-Run 'attempted'/'hookFailed' maps reset on no-candidate path. 3. execute_bead_loop.go:483-508 preflight rejection becomes per-bead continue, not loop exit. 4. loop.end event includes structured exit_reason field. Recognized values include 'sigterm', 'sigint', 'fatal_config', 'once_complete', 'explicit_poll_zero'; 'providers_exhausted' slot reserved for ddx-aede917d. 5. Server-managed workers report no_ready_work as transient (worker stays in 'running' state with 'idle' substate); existing 'exited' status reserved for true exits per AC #4. 6. Tests: TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue (poll-interval > 0; verify loop stays alive across N empty polls); TestLoop_PreflightRejectionContinues (one bead's preflight failure doesn't abandon siblings); TestLoop_OnceFlagStillExits (back-compat); TestLoop_ExplicitPollZeroExits (back-compat). 7. Manual verification: spawn worker via 'ddx work --harness claude'; let queue drain; verify worker stays running > 5 min after queue empty (vs current behavior of immediate exit). 8. Update FEAT-006 / FEAT-010 docs noting the new long-running default + the explicit opt-out flags.",
.ddx/executions/20260502T204356-13980032/prompt.md:5:Workers exit prematurely even when ready beads remain in the queue. Operators have to manually relaunch, defeating the point of a long-running drain.

## Root causes (from sub-agent investigation)

1. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:440-441**: when nextCandidate returns no eligible bead AND runtime.PollInterval <= 0, the loop returns immediately. Combined with the per-Run 'attempted' map (line 388, 476), once each currently-ready bead has been *attempted once* the loop exits even though those same beads (or new arrivals) are still queue-ready.

2. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:483-508**: routing-preflight rejection on a single bead exits the entire loop ('return result, nil'), abandoning all other ready beads.

3. **Default --poll-interval=0 everywhere**: cli/cmd/work.go:66, cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1568, cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:115, cli/internal/server/server.go:2281. All three recent server-spawned workers in .ddx/workers/ show poll_interval: '0s', so cause #1 fires the first empty poll.

## Fix shape (locked decisions baked in)

- Flip default --poll-interval from 0 → **30s** everywhere (CLI + server adapter). Operator can override with --once for one-shot or explicit --poll-interval=N.
- Reset per-Run 'attempted'/'hookFailed' maps when the loop falls through to 'no candidate', before sleeping. Prevents the 'attempted-once = exit' trap.
- Convert preflight early-exit (483-508) to per-bead 'continue' so one bead's routing failure doesn't abandon the rest.
- Add structured exit_reason to loop.end event; reserve 'providers_exhausted' slot for ddx-aede917d (quota-pause work).
- Server-managed workers expose 'no_ready_work' as a **transient 'running (idle)' substate**, not terminal. Worker stays alive; UI shows running (idle).
- Preserve --once and explicit --poll-interval=0 for back-compat (legacy 'drain-and-exit' semantics).

Acceptable exit conditions remain (per existing beads):
- SIGTERM / SIGINT (operator-driven)
- All providers durably out (per ddx-aede917d quota pause/resume — workers pause+probe, not exit)
- No providers configured at all (fatal config error)
- --once explicitly requested
- --poll-interval=0 explicitly requested

.ddx/executions/20260503T021424-dac100b6/manifest.json:13:    "description": "Operator-observed gap: when ddx-server restarts, in-flight worker subprocesses lose their connection to the orchestrator. The current model has two execution paths (--local vs server-spawned) that diverge in lifecycle management, configuration plumbing (the dropped-flags class — see ddx-29058e2a), and observability (server can see only what it spawned).

Proposed architectural shift: WORKERS ARE LONG-LIVED PROCESSES THAT PHONE HOME TO THE SERVER VIA API.

DESIGN PROPOSALS (this TD chooses among them)

PROPOSAL A — Server orchestrates, workers are long-lived clients
- Worker subprocess starts (either via `ddx work` CLI or via server-spawned)
- Worker registers with server (via /api/workers/register, gets worker-id + heartbeat token)
- Worker polls /api/workers/<id>/next-bead via long-poll or websocket; receives bead spec from server
- Worker reports progress via /api/workers/<id>/event {kind, body}
- Worker reports completion via /api/workers/<id>/result {bead-id, status, evidence}
- If server restarts: worker's heartbeat fails → worker enters "disconnected" state, finishes current bead, reconnects on server return
- If worker dies: server's heartbeat-timeout reclaims the bead

PROPOSAL B — Server is optional sidecar, worker is autonomous
- Worker runs independently; no server required
- If a server is reachable (via .ddx/server.addr), worker emits events to it best-effort
- Server is a passive observer, not an orchestrator
- Restart-safe by construction (worker doesn't depend on server)
- Loses: server-driven label filters, dynamic priority overrides, centralized cancel

PROPOSAL C — Hybrid: workers are autonomous executors but yield orchestration to server when present
- Worker checks for .ddx/server.addr at startup
- If present: registers as managed; receives bead assignments from server's queue
- If absent or unreachable: runs autonomously using local bead store
- Server restart: managed workers detect via heartbeat failure; gracefully complete current bead, then either retry registration OR fall back to autonomous mode (configurable)

UNIFIED API CONTRACT (all proposals)

POST /api/workers/register { project_root, harness, model_pref, label_filter, capabilities[] } → { worker_id, session_token, heartbeat_interval_ms }
POST /api/workers/<id>/heartbeat { state: idle|claiming|executing|reviewing } → { server_command: continue|pause|drain|terminate }
GET  /api/workers/<id>/next-bead → { bead, attempt_id, base_rev } | { wait_for_seconds: N }
POST /api/workers/<id>/event { kind, body, bead_id } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/result { bead_id, attempt_id, status, evidence_dir, commit_sha } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/disconnect → 204 (graceful)

The existing --local mode collapses to: a worker that registers with itself (no server) using an in-process implementation of the API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

This TD intersects:
- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): the spec becomes the registration payload — single struct still applies

- ddx-4c51d33e (cross-project leak): workers identify their project at registration; server enforces project boundaries via session token
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): becomes natural — worker stays alive, polls for work, doesn't exit on empty queue

1. Define API contract + handlers (cli/internal/server/worker_api.go)
2. Add worker client (cli/internal/agent/work/client.go) implementing the API
3. Add in-process API implementation for --local mode (cli/internal/agent/work/local_api.go)
4. Migrate execute_bead_loop / drain_loop to consume the API instead of direct store access
5. Migrate server worker spawn to register-then-poll instead of inline-execution

NOT IN SCOPE FOR THIS TD

- Multi-server federation (each server owns its workers; cross-server is a separate concern)
- Auth beyond session tokens (the worker-server channel is local; no remote workers in v1)

.ddx/executions/20260503T021424-dac100b6/prompt.md:5:Operator-observed gap: when ddx-server restarts, in-flight worker subprocesses lose their connection to the orchestrator. The current model has two execution paths (--local vs server-spawned) that diverge in lifecycle management, configuration plumbing (the dropped-flags class — see ddx-29058e2a), and observability (server can see only what it spawned).

Proposed architectural shift: WORKERS ARE LONG-LIVED PROCESSES THAT PHONE HOME TO THE SERVER VIA API.

DESIGN PROPOSALS (this TD chooses among them)

PROPOSAL A — Server orchestrates, workers are long-lived clients
- Worker subprocess starts (either via `ddx work` CLI or via server-spawned)
- Worker registers with server (via /api/workers/register, gets worker-id + heartbeat token)
- Worker polls /api/workers/<id>/next-bead via long-poll or websocket; receives bead spec from server
- Worker reports progress via /api/workers/<id>/event {kind, body}
- Worker reports completion via /api/workers/<id>/result {bead-id, status, evidence}
- If server restarts: worker's heartbeat fails → worker enters "disconnected" state, finishes current bead, reconnects on server return
- If worker dies: server's heartbeat-timeout reclaims the bead

PROPOSAL B — Server is optional sidecar, worker is autonomous
- Worker runs independently; no server required
- If a server is reachable (via .ddx/server.addr), worker emits events to it best-effort
- Server is a passive observer, not an orchestrator
- Restart-safe by construction (worker doesn't depend on server)
- Loses: server-driven label filters, dynamic priority overrides, centralized cancel

PROPOSAL C — Hybrid: workers are autonomous executors but yield orchestration to server when present
- Worker checks for .ddx/server.addr at startup
- If present: registers as managed; receives bead assignments from server's queue
- If absent or unreachable: runs autonomously using local bead store
- Server restart: managed workers detect via heartbeat failure; gracefully complete current bead, then either retry registration OR fall back to autonomous mode (configurable)

UNIFIED API CONTRACT (all proposals)

POST /api/workers/register { project_root, harness, model_pref, label_filter, capabilities[] } → { worker_id, session_token, heartbeat_interval_ms }
POST /api/workers/<id>/heartbeat { state: idle|claiming|executing|reviewing } → { server_command: continue|pause|drain|terminate }
GET  /api/workers/<id>/next-bead → { bead, attempt_id, base_rev } | { wait_for_seconds: N }
POST /api/workers/<id>/event { kind, body, bead_id } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/result { bead_id, attempt_id, status, evidence_dir, commit_sha } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/disconnect → 204 (graceful)

The existing --local mode collapses to: a worker that registers with itself (no server) using an in-process implementation of the API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

.ddx/executions/20260502T205137-13829788/manifest.json:13:    "description": "Workers exit prematurely even when ready beads remain in the queue. Operators have to manually relaunch, defeating the point of a long-running drain.

## Root causes (from sub-agent investigation)

1. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:440-441**: when nextCandidate returns no eligible bead AND runtime.PollInterval <= 0, the loop returns immediately. Combined with the per-Run 'attempted' map (line 388, 476), once each currently-ready bead has been *attempted once* the loop exits even though those same beads (or new arrivals) are still queue-ready.

2. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:483-508**: routing-preflight rejection on a single bead exits the entire loop ('return result, nil'), abandoning all other ready beads.

3. **Default --poll-interval=0 everywhere**: cli/cmd/work.go:66, cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1568, cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:115, cli/internal/server/server.go:2281. All three recent server-spawned workers in .ddx/workers/ show poll_interval: '0s', so cause #1 fires the first empty poll.

## Fix shape (locked decisions baked in)

- Flip default --poll-interval from 0 → **30s** everywhere (CLI + server adapter). Operator can override with --once for one-shot or explicit --poll-interval=N.
- Reset per-Run 'attempted'/'hookFailed' maps when the loop falls through to 'no candidate', before sleeping. Prevents the 'attempted-once = exit' trap.
- Convert preflight early-exit (483-508) to per-bead 'continue' so one bead's routing failure doesn't abandon the rest.
- Add structured exit_reason to loop.end event; reserve 'providers_exhausted' slot for ddx-aede917d (quota-pause work).
- Server-managed workers expose 'no_ready_work' as a **transient 'running (idle)' substate**, not terminal. Worker stays alive; UI shows running (idle).
- Preserve --once and explicit --poll-interval=0 for back-compat (legacy 'drain-and-exit' semantics).

Acceptable exit conditions remain (per existing beads):
- SIGTERM / SIGINT (operator-driven)
- All providers durably out (per ddx-aede917d quota pause/resume — workers pause+probe, not exit)
- No providers configured at all (fatal config error)
- --once explicitly requested
- --poll-interval=0 explicitly requested",

    - cli/internal/server/server.go:2328          (pollInterval := 30 * time.Second)

    bead AND poll-interval > 0, the loop now emits loop.idle, resets the

- All providers durably out (per ddx-aede917d quota pause/resume — workers pause+probe, not exit)

## Why this bead was not advanced in one execute-bead pass

The bead asks for `ddx work` to auto-route execution without any project
config. Read literally, the eight acceptance criteria require:

  AC1  Zero-config drain succeeds (project-side change + upstream relax)
  AC2  Reword the under-specified error to point at the global config
  AC3  Enable the triage complexity gate by default + opt-out flag
  AC4  Auto-tier selection from bead metadata (labels/kind/scope)
  AC5  Coarse-bead handling: decompose, OR emit a NEW `needs_triage`
       execution status (distinct from `execution_failed`)
  AC6  Failure recovery: retry-in-tier vs escalate-to-next-tier
  AC7  New integration test with a temp project + minimal global config
  AC8  Doc update presenting zero-config flow as the default

Each of AC3, AC4, and AC5 is a feature-sized piece of work on its own:

  - AC4 (auto-tier selection) implements a tier-inference engine over
    bead metadata. There is no such engine today; routing currently
    accepts operator intent or rejects with `routing under-specified`.
    This overlaps directly with the open endpoint-first routing redesign
    (memory: project_endpoint_routing_design — 2026-04-21: kill
    named-provider profiles, move to endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy
    model match). Implementing tier inference now without aligning with
    that redesign would either be redone or block it.

  - AC5 (`needs_triage` status) introduces a new ExecuteBeadStatus value
    that cuts across:
      internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go     (status enum + classify)
      internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go       (loop handling + retry)
      internal/bead/store.go                    (close vs unclaim policy)
      cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop.go             (operator output)
      docs/                                     (status table)
    plus the matching test surface. This cannot share a commit with the
    routing fix without conflating two reviewable changes.

  - AC3 (triage gate default-on) is a small toggle, but the gate
    function `w.ComplexityGate` is currently injected from outside and
    is `nil` in the CLI path (internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:506).
    Enabling it by default requires either a default ComplexityGate
    implementation (which is itself a dispatcher into AC4 inference) or
    a stub that always returns `shouldClaim=true` — the latter is
    indistinguishable from disabled and would not satisfy the AC.

The `routing under-specified` error itself is enforced in the upstream
fizeau library at:

  $GOMODCACHE/.../fizeau@v0.9.28/service_execute.go:192

DDx is currently on github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau v0.9.28. The
short-circuit fires when *all* of Harness, Profile, ModelRef, Model,
Provider are empty. AC1 cannot be met purely on the DDx side without
either (a) populating one of those fields from a sensible default
(which is AC4) or (b) a fizeau release that lets the engine's
ResolveRoute see a "global default_provider" hint. Per memory
(feedback_no_local_replace), DDx must always import a properly tagged
agent release, so a `replace` directive is not available even
temporarily.

## What was verified before stopping

  - The under-specified error string is upstream-only — it does not
    occur in any DDx Go file (Grep across cli/, internal/, cmd/).
  - The flag set on `ddx agent execute-loop` already exposes --profile,
    --provider, --harness, --model, --model-ref, --effort. The user's
    report shows `--profile cheap` still failing, which is a separate
    bug from the bead's main thrust: profile is set as a non-empty
    flag default (agent.DefaultRoutingProfile, agent_cmd.go:1563) but
    is being passed through `singleTierAttempt` which uses
    CLIOverrides without setting Profile (agent_cmd.go:1716–1725).
    That single-line gap is plausibly the proximate cause of the
    `--profile cheap` regression but solving it does not satisfy the
    broader bead.
  - Triage gate disabled-by-default is at execute_bead_loop.go:506;
    the gate is a function field on ExecuteBeadWorker. There is no
    in-tree default implementation.

## Suggested decomposition

Recommend splitting this bead into the following children before any
implementation pass attempts AC1:

  ddx-XXXX  Profile flag passthrough fix: ensure --profile/--provider/
            --model-ref reach singleTierAttempt's CLIOverrides
            (single small commit + regression test). Likely closes the
            `--profile cheap` workaround failure.

  ddx-XXXX  Improve under-specified error: when global agent config has
            providers but the request has no routing inputs, point the
            operator at the global default_provider rather than at the
            per-project flag list. Coordinate with fizeau release.

  ddx-XXXX  Tier inference engine over bead metadata
            (kind/labels/file-touch estimate). Aligns with the
            endpoint-first routing redesign.

  ddx-XXXX  ExecuteBeadStatusNeedsTriage status + classify rules +
            close/unclaim policy + tests.

  ddx-XXXX  Default-on ComplexityGate implementation (depends on tier
            inference) + opt-out flag --no-triage-gate.

  ddx-XXXX  Zero-config integration test (depends on the above).

  ddx-XXXX  Doc update: getting-started zero-config flow.

## What a follow-up attempt needs

If a single follow-up attempt is desired, it should narrow the bead to
just AC1 + AC2 with an explicit hand-off: the agent picks a default
harness/provider from the global agent config when nothing else is set,
treating the absence of project config as "use global default_provider"
rather than as a hard error. AC3–AC8 should be filed as sibling beads.

Per the bead's own AC #5, a coarse bead that no tier can confidently
execute is supposed to surface for operator triage rather than burn a
dispatch slot. This rationale is exactly that signal.",

  cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/plugins/+page.svelte
  lines 56-60: dispatchingPlugins Set, inFlightWorkers Map<name,workerId>,
  inFlightActions, dispatchSnapshots, workerFailures.
  lines 115-117: isBusy(name) gate.
  lines 458-477: Install/Update buttons disabled while busy.

AC2 (worker-driven refresh):
  +page.svelte lines 225-238 (subscribeToWorker), 240-258 (handleWorkerTerminal).
  On non-failure terminal phase, refreshPlugins() runs, then clearInFlight(name).

AC3 (fallback polling):
  +page.svelte lines 190-223. scheduleFallbackPolling sets a 30s timer
  (configurable via __ddxPluginFallbackDelayMs). When it fires without a
  terminal event, startFallbackPolling polls every 2s until the snapshot
  changes. Playwright test at e2e/plugins.spec.ts:349 ("plugin update falls
  back to polling when worker stream drops terminal event") exercises this.

AC4 (in-flight visual state):
  +page.svelte lines 377-383. Loader2 spinner + "Installing..."/"Updating..."
  label replaces the status badge while workerId && action are set. Button
  disabled with Tooltip pointing to worker.

AC5 (failure surfacing):
  +page.svelte lines 392-405. workerFailures Map stores {workerId, action}
  on failure phases; renders red AlertCircle badge with link to worker page
  and title "Update failed"/"Install failed". handleWorkerTerminal (line 247)
  returns early on failure phases without calling refreshPlugins, preserving
  pre-dispatch state. dispatchError remains for synchronous mutation errors
  only (lines 287-289, 338-343).

AC6 (concurrent dispatch):
  Map-keyed tracking allows independent state per plugin. Header at
  +page.svelte lines 318-335 iterates inFlightWorkers.entries() and renders
  one link per active worker. Playwright test at e2e/plugins.spec.ts:381
  ("two plugin updates track separate workers concurrently").

AC7 (no extra refetch overhead):
  refreshPlugins (+page.svelte:184-188) calls only PLUGINS_LIST_QUERY, not
  the full page data loader.

AC8 (Playwright e2e):
  cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/plugins.spec.ts has dedicated tests
  covering in-flight state (line 284 "plugin update refreshes card when
  worker completes successfully"), failure (line 314), polling fallback
  (line 349), concurrent (line 381). Uses page.routeWebSocket to simulate
  the workerProgress subscription.

AC9 (no regressions):
  Install flow through ConfirmDialog uses the same dispatchPlugin() code
  path (lines 295-301), so install also gets in-flight tracking.
  Existing install test at e2e/plugins.spec.ts:199 (US-098.b) still asserts
  the Install flow.

  cli/internal/server/workers.go:393-519 (StartPluginAction + runPluginAction)

  TestWorkerManagerStartPluginActionPublishesTerminalProgress and
  TestWorkerManagerStartPluginActionPublishesFailureProgress

.ddx/executions/20260423T043550-7e9242ea/result.json:3:  "rationale": "**No resolver changes.** `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go` is untouched. AC1 requires `EfficacyRows` to read from the sharded sessions index and remove `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot`, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo`.
**No fixture or benchmarks.** AC3/AC4 require a ≥10k-row fixture across 2+ shards with baseline latency measurements. Nothing seeds data or measures performance.
**No tests of any kind.** AC6 (completeness/superset), AC7 (freshness), and AC8 (Playwright smoke) all require new test files. None are present.
**No schema changes.** AC2 requires optional `since`, `until`, `projectId` filter args on the GraphQL query. No `.graphql` or resolver signature changes appear.
**The result.json itself reports `task_succeeded`**, but the merge commit (`41fa9657`) it references only contains "test(efficacy): add real-backend HTTP smoke + align UI fixture" per git log — suggesting partial work may exist on a branch that wasn't included in this diff. The next agent should check whether commit `41fa9657` contains unreflected work, or whether the execution truly produced no implementation."

data.text: I found the ADR references; next I’m reading the exact decision language and the surrounding work-plan text so I can tell whether this bead is a documentation-only coordination update or already resolved upstream.

data.output:
Rev 4 dramatically simplifies sequencing. The architecture is additive:
the worker keeps working as it does today; server-side reporting is new
machinery that sits alongside. There is no "freeze C5/C7/C9 until X"
rule because nothing about the worker state machine changes.

- **`ddx-29058e2a` (ExecuteLoopSpec unification)** — independent. Rev 4
  does NOT make ExecuteLoopSpec a wire format. The bead remains valuable
  as a worker-side struct unification (cobra → in-process flow) and
  should land independently.
- **`ddx-5cb6e6cd` C5 (no_changes adjudication)** — NOT FROZEN. Rev 5
  doesn't reshape no_changes generation; mirroring stays an observer.
- **`ddx-5cb6e6cd` C7 (uniform Guard contract; delete attempted/hookFailed maps)** — **COORDINATE.** Direct conflict: rev 5's worker keeps the per-Run `attempted` map (preserved from commit `41cb762e`); C7 wants to delete it. Resolution: C7 lands BEFORE rev 5 implementation work, OR rev 5 implementation work coordinates with C7's replacement (Guard-derived exclusion). Until coordinated, C7 stays parked.
- **`ddx-5cb6e6cd` C9 (StopCondition + cost-cap)** — NOT FROZEN. The autonomous worker keeps the stay-alive defaults; C9's StopCondition becomes an internal worker construct, server sees terminal state via the result event.
- **`ddx-dc157075` (stay-alive at commit `41cb762e`)** — already shipped
  and preserved. Worker's autonomous behavior plus 30s poll-interval
  default carry forward unchanged.
- **`ddx-4c51d33e` (cross-project leak at commit `33b97f25`)** — already
  shipped. Rev 4 prevents recurrence at the worker level (one worker
  per project bead store; no cross-store reads).
- **`ddx-9d55601f` (picker priority bug at commit `80f51574`)** — picker
  diagnostic events stay; rev 4 explicitly mirrors them to the server
  endpoint when the worker is Connected.
- **`ddx-9e4c238d` (auto-routing rejection bug, just filed)** —
  independent. Fixing DDx's pre-flight to pass through to fizeau is
  orthogonal to the autonomous-default architecture. Should land in
  parallel.
- **`ddx-1d867ec1` (rename `execute_bead_loop.go`)** — coordinate. The
  rename should align with whatever package layout the worker uses
  post-rev-4. Probably stays as `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`
  for now since rev 4 doesn't reshape the worker package; revisit when
  C13 lands.
- **`ddx-076147ee` (this TD)** — closes when the rev 4 ADR is approved
  and the implementation roadmap below is filed as beads.
- **`ddx-50da9674` (clean fixture repo)** — supports rev 4's acceptance
  test; independent.

## Implementation roadmap

Rev 5 expands from rev 4's 5 beads to 9, after codex flagged missing
UI/doctor/FEAT-006/server-spawn-migration/backfill scope.

1. **server: ingestion endpoints** — implement `POST /api/workers/register` + `POST /api/workers/<id>/event` + `POST /api/workers/<id>/backfill`; in-memory derived view backed by append-only `.ddx/server/worker-events.jsonl`; freshness state machine (connected/stale/disconnected); requireTrusted boundary. ~300 LOC.

2. **worker: server-probe goroutine + best-effort event mirror** — periodic reachability check on `~/.local/share/ddx/server.addr` (XDG-compliant, matches current code) with jittered 30s interval, immediate first probe, exponential backoff on consecutive failures; transitions emit register/disconnect; in-memory ring buffer for backfill (200-event cap); event POSTs are best-effort. ~250 LOC.

3. **server: operator-cancel** — `/api/beads/<id>/cancel` writes `extra.cancel-requested: true`; worker mid-attempt poll (10s default) checks bead `extra` and aborts at next safe point with `preserved_for_review` reason `operator_cancel`; idempotency via `cancel-honored: true`. ~150 LOC.

4. **CLI: deprecate `--local`** — flag becomes no-op with deprecation warning; update CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, getting-started, and the cobra help text. Existing tests asserting `--local`-specific behavior get rewritten or deleted. (Codex notes the sweep is larger than 50 LOC: `cli/cmd/work_test.go`, `agent_execute_loop_test.go`, `zero_config_work_test.go`, `skills/ddx/reference/work.md`, demos.) ~150 LOC + doc edits.

5. **server: derived-view GraphQL + UI workers panel** — schema fields: `workers { id, project, harness, state, last_event_at, mirror_failures_count, had_dropped_backfill, current_bead, current_attempt }`; UI surfaces freshness indicators + duplicate-worker display + the "trusted-peer reported, not authoritative" labeling. Existing workers panel migrated. ~400 LOC frontend + ~100 LOC backend.

6. **`ddx agent doctor` migration** — read worker state from server's runtime registry when available; fall back to `.ddx/workers/` on-disk files when no server. The on-disk format stays as the fallback-source-of-truth for one alpha release lag, then deprecated. ~150 LOC.

7. **server-spawn path migration** — `cli/internal/server/workers.go` and `handleStartExecuteLoopWorker` switch from hand-marshalled spec to `exec ddx work` with env-vars. The legacy spec serialization deletes when this lands. ~200 LOC + test updates.

8. **FEAT-006 amendment** — replace the rev 3 worker-contract section with rev 5's autonomous-default + best-effort-mirror description. Drop session-token/heartbeat/`next-bead`/result/disconnect contract; add register/event/backfill/cancel + freshness state. ~200 lines docs.

9. **acceptance + soak** — uses `ddx-50da9674` clean fixture repo. Multi-worker drain WITH server; restart server mid-flight; verify worker continues + reconnects + backfills. Then drain with NO server; verify identical behavior. Operator-cancel test (write marker, observe `preserved_for_review` with `operator_cancel` reason within SLA). Soak: ≥1 week of normal operator use. Final bead.

Plus one COORDINATION bead, NOT new work but a parking-lot for the
C7 conflict:

10. **coordinate: C7 (Guard contract, delete attempted map) with rev 5 worker** — either C7 lands first and rev 5 implementation uses Guard-derived exclusion, OR C7 stays parked until rev 5's worker code defines its replacement for the per-Run attempted map. Decision documented; no LOC.

Total estimated effort: 2-4 weeks (vs 1-2 weeks for rev 4's understated 5-bead roadmap, vs 8-10 weeks for rev 3's design).

## Consequences

- **Workers don't depend on the server.** The headline operator
  requirement (server restart preserves in-flight work) is satisfied
  by construction: workers don't notice server restarts as anything
  more than transient mirror failures.
- **No `--local` flag.** Removed from the CLI surface. Behavior of
  today's `--local` becomes the always-on default. Backward compat
  preserved for one release via a no-op flag with deprecation warning.

  ddx-d7aca866 is a rollup/tracking epic for ADR-022 rev 5. Its acceptance criteria
  are not directly implementable in a single agent attempt:

    - "All 9 implementation children closed (steps 1-9 from the roadmap)"
    - "Coordination bead 10 closed (C7 conflict resolved)"
    - "FEAT-006 reflects rev 5"
    - "Acceptance bead's soak passed for ≥1 week"  ← calendar-time gate
    - "CHANGELOG entry merged"

  Closure is gated on (a) each child bead being independently executed and closed,
  and (b) a ≥1-week soak window that cannot be compressed by an agent. The epic
  itself has no code/test deliverable; it is a rollup whose status should flip to
  closed only after the last child closes and the soak window elapses.

  Current child inventory (queried via `ddx bead list --json | jq 'parent==epic'`):
    - ddx-2892f2cf  open   step 1: server ingestion endpoints (register + event + backfill)

  the coordination bead (step 10 / C7 resolution) have not been created. Filing

  done by a human or a dedicated decomposition pass against the ADR roadmap —
  not auto-generated mid-execution loop, because the ADR-022 rev 5 roadmap
  sections name specific contracts (auth, schema, retry, ack semantics) that
  warrant an explicit triage review before bead text is committed.

    2. Create beads for steps 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9 and the coordination bead
       (step 10 / C7), each as a child of ddx-d7aca866 via `ddx bead dep add`.

       once they all close and the soak/CHANGELOG ACs are satisfied.

  The drain loop tracks attempted and hookFailed beads via ad-hoc in-memory maps rather than a structured Guard contract, making the loop body untestable in isolation and the skip policy implicit.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413 declares attempted := make(map[string]struct{}) and hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{}). Lines 534-544 manually clear both maps between sleep cycles. Lines 591-601 implement the two-strikes pre-claim hook policy by mutating these maps inline. nextCandidate (line 1216) accepts attempted as a parameter and checks membership at line 1223. No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Define Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go: Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, reason string).
  - Implement two concrete Guards: PreClaimGuard (wraps runtime.PreClaimHook two-strikes logic) and ComplexityGuard (wraps w.ComplexityGate nil-check).
  - Delete attempted and hookFailed maps from execute_bead_loop.go:403-413. Replace nextCandidate's attempted parameter with drain.result.Results slice (already populated per-iteration): filter beads whose ID appears in Results.
  - Pre-claim hook denial: call w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, 30s, ...) instead of recording in hookFailed.
  - Add TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips and TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce to a new cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Any change to SetExecutionCooldown duration policy (controlled by caller constants).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: guard denials should skip the candidate rather than wedge the queue.
- P2: each guard owns only its own skip policy.
- P4: a denied bead stays isolated from other beads.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","fallback_chain":[],"actual_power":8}","created_at":"2026-05-06T01:15:24.182722714Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260506T005752-1f271178","harness":"codex","model":"gpt-5.4-mini","input_tokens":13289821,"output_tokens":42190,"total_tokens":13332011,"cost_usd":0,"duration_ms":1049699,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-06T01:15:24.410294523Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=13332011 model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"2026-05-06T01:15:30.858136676Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"erik","body":"failure_class=review-error: unparseable

3. Concurrent-worker test: TestExecuteLoop_TwoWorkersBothClaimP0sBeforeP2s — two workers running against a queue of 2×P0 + 4×P2 each claim a P0; only after both P0s are taken do they pick P2.

6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"9cf40e4eb13ff019c60924b51f5eab50d9ce9c22","created_at":"2026-05-02T23:46:55.656187355Z","description":"OBSERVED
Two ddx workers (one server-managed, one --local --harness=claude) repeatedly claim P2 beads while four ready P0 beads sit unclaimed at the top of the queue.

REPRO (2026-05-02)
- ddx bead ready returns (top of list):
  - ddx-dc157075 P0
  - ddx-a946c744 P0 (triaged but child ddx-aee651ec P0 ready)
  - ddx-aee651ec P0
  - ddx-29058e2a P0
- Workers claim:
  - ddx-6d132f62 P2 (Story 16)
  - ddx-a7e7a1a1 P2 (Story 15-3)
  - ddx-297f840c P2 (Story 6 frontend) — earlier worker
- The 4 P0 beads have NO blocker:
  - extra.execute-loop-retry-after: null
  - extra.execution-eligible: null (defaults to true)
  - extra.superseded-by: null
  - all deps closed
- They appear in ddx bead ready AND should appear in Store.ReadyExecution() with priority=0 sorted first.

CODE PATH (per investigation)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1145 nextCandidate → calls Store.ReadyExecution()
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1246 ReadyExecution → readyFiltered(true)
- store.go:1357 calls sortBeadsForQueue(ready) which sorts by priority asc (0 first; verified at store.go:1462, 1475)
- Loop returns first candidate not in attempted map (loop.go:1150-1158)

By design this MUST return P0 first. Yet workers are getting P2. Hypotheses:
1. The `attempted` map persists across what should be fresh worker runs (worker process lifecycle bug — possibly related to ddx-dc157075).
2. A label filter is silently set on the worker spec (the server-managed worker had empty harness/model — possibly empty label filter is treated as a regex that excludes some labels).
3. Two workers race on Claim() and the loser falls through, retrying nextCandidate which now sees the bead in attempted and skips to lower-priority.
4. The server-managed worker uses a different picker path than ExecuteBeadWorker.nextCandidate (e.g., workers.go has its own selection that ignores priority).
5. The P0 beads have a label/area excluded by some default filter the operator doesn't see.

INVESTIGATION NEEDED
- Trace each currently-running worker: print its full spec (harness, model, labelFilter, oncce, pollInterval).
- Print the result of ReadyExecution() on demand (add a `ddx server workers next-candidate <worker-id>` debug command, or run from REPL).
- Add structured event when a worker passes over a P0 to claim a P2 — log the reason (in attempted, label-filtered, claim race, etc.).
- Inspect cli/internal/server/workers.go for any divergence from execute_bead_loop.go's nextCandidate.

LIKELY RELATED
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive fix) — workers exiting prematurely and the persisted `attempted` map across restarts could explain skips.
- ddx-29058e2a (server flag-drop) — if the spec is corrupted en route, label filter could be set incorrectly.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Changing the priority sort order itself.
- Adding new picker dimensions (story affinity, area concurrency caps).
- Refactoring the picker into a service (separate concern).","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","fallback_chain":[]}","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:01:06.607642763Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260503T015741-138c36ea","harness":"claude","input_tokens":22,"output_tokens":5623,"total_tokens":5645,"cost_usd":0.6801000000000001,"duration_ms":187106,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:01:06.640102563Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=5645 cost_usd=0.6801"},{"actor":"erik","body":"Bead ddx-9d55601f was already implemented and merged in commit 80f51574
("fix(agent): emit picker.priority_skip + claim_race diagnostic events
[ddx-9d55601f]"), which is reachable from the current HEAD (9cf40e4e).

Status of acceptance criteria against HEAD:

AC #1 — Root cause + investigation report.
  Report at .ddx/executions/20260502T231414-92c45184/picker-priority-bug.md
  (the previous attempt's run-id). Conclusion: the picker is not
  violating priority ordering; the operator's "starved" P0s were on
  extra.execute-loop-retry-after cooldown and correctly excluded by
  Store.ReadyExecution()/readyFiltered. Full call-graph trace and
  per-hypothesis evaluation included.

AC #2 — TestExecuteLoop_ClaimsHighestPriorityFirst exists at
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_priority_test.go.

AC #3 — TestExecuteLoop_TwoWorkersBothClaimP0sBeforeP2s exists at
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_priority_test.go.

AC #4 — Two diagnostic events added in execute_bead_loop.go:
  - picker.priority_skip with reasons {in_attempted, label_filter}
    (eligibility_filter, retry_cooldown reserved in vocabulary; those
