.ddx/beads.jsonl:910:{"acceptance":"1. cli/cmd/work_plan.go implements 'ddx work plan' as a cobra subcommand of 'ddx work'. Help text describes the relationship to 'ddx work' (preview vs execute) and 'ddx bead ready' (picker-aware vs ready-set).

2. Picker filter+sort logic extracted from cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go nextCandidate into a reusable function PreviewQueue(filters PickerFilters) []QueueEntry. ExecuteBeadWorker.nextCandidate now calls PreviewQueue + skips attempted-map members; behavior unchanged.

3. 'ddx work plan' default output: top 10 ready+eligible beads in picker order, with columns Position, Bead ID, Priority, Updated, Status, Filter-decision, Why.

4. 'ddx work plan --limit=0' returns the full queue including skip reasons for non-eligible beads.

5. 'ddx work plan --label-filter=phase:2,area:agent' filters identically to the worker's label intersection.

6. 'ddx work plan --capabilities=bead-attempt' filters identically to the worker's capability matching (when capabilities are implemented; today this is a no-op pass-through).

7. 'ddx work plan --json' emits an ordered JSON array with the same fields, suitable for piping to jq.

8. WIRED-IN: 'ddx work plan' is registered as a subcommand under 'ddx work' (or as a sibling top-level if cobra doesn't naturally support subcommands of leaf commands — verify the existing 'ddx work' structure first). Verified by 'ddx work plan --help' output AND by 'git grep "planCmd\|NewPlanCommand\|workPlanCmd" cli/cmd/' showing the registration. Worker code path imports and calls the extracted PreviewQueue function — verified by 'git grep PreviewQueue cli/internal/agent/' showing the call site.

9. Tests in cli/cmd/work_plan_test.go AND cli/internal/agent/preview_queue_test.go:
   - TestPreviewQueue_PrioritySort — fixture queue P0+P1+P2 mixed; assert P0 first, FIFO within priority
   - TestPreviewQueue_LabelFilter — fixture queue with mixed labels; assert filter intersection narrows correctly
   - TestPreviewQueue_CooldownSkip — fixture bead with execute-loop-retry-after in future; assert skip reason names the time
   - TestPreviewQueue_DepsBlocked — fixture bead with unclosed dep; assert skip reason names the dep
   - TestPreviewQueue_DeterministicAcrossRuns — call PreviewQueue twice with same inputs; assert byte-identical output (no per-Run state leaking in)
   - TestWorkPlan_CLI_TextOutput — assert default text format matches the documented columns
   - TestWorkPlan_CLI_JSONOutput — assert --json output is valid JSON parseable by jq
   - TestPicker_StillWorks_AfterRefactor — assert ExecuteBeadWorker.nextCandidate still returns the first eligible candidate identically to pre-refactor behavior

10. Quality gates:
   - cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...
   - lefthook run pre-commit

11. Conventional commit message ending [<this-bead-id>]. Stage cli/cmd/work_plan*.go + cli/internal/agent/preview_queue.go + cli/cmd/work.go (subcommand registration line) + the modified cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (only the nextCandidate extraction). DO NOT use git add -A.

12. Manual verification (operator runs after merge):
   - 'ddx work plan' shows current top-of-queue with priorities P0 first
   - 'ddx work plan --limit=0 --json | jq '.[].id'' lists every bead in determined picker order
   - Comparing 'ddx work plan' output to actual ddx work claim events should show identical bead IDs in identical order","created_at":"2026-05-03T20:07:58.299023187Z","description":"PROBLEM
Operators cannot inspect what 'ddx work' would pick without actually picking. When an unexpected bead claims (or an expected bead is skipped), the only diagnostic is the diagnostic events emitted AFTER claim attempt — by then the operator has already lost the chance to intervene. There is no read-only 'show me your decision' surface.

ROOT CAUSE
The picker logic at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1145 (ExecuteBeadWorker.nextCandidate) reads the bead store via Store.ReadyExecution (cli/internal/bead/store.go:1246) and applies filters (per-Run attempted map, label_filter intersection) before returning the first eligible candidate. The picker is invoked only INSIDE the loop — there is no standalone API to query 'what would you pick?'.

The existing 'ddx bead ready' command returns ready beads but does NOT apply the picker's per-Run attempted filter (which is non-deterministic across runs anyway), nor does it visualize the picker's tie-breaking rules (priority asc, then updated_at asc per FIFO).

Bead metadata IS deterministic. Given the bead store + stable filter inputs (label_filter, capabilities), the picker's output for the FIRST eligible bead must be 100% deterministic. There is no reason this shouldn't be inspectable interactively.

NAMING NOTE
Originally proposed as 'ddx queue plan'. Renamed to 'ddx work plan' because 'queue' is not an established concept in the CLI surface; 'work' is (the existing 'ddx work' drain command). This makes the new subcommand a natural sibling: 'ddx work' executes the drain; 'ddx work plan' previews what the drain would do.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a new subcommand at cli/cmd/work_plan.go (sibling file to cli/cmd/work.go):

  ddx work plan [--label-filter=<labels>] [--capabilities=<csv>] [--limit=<N>] [--json]

Behavior:
1. Read the bead store via the same Store.ReadyExecution path the worker uses (so filters like cooldown, superseded, execution-eligible apply identically).
2. Apply the same sort the worker applies: priority asc, then updated_at asc within priority.
3. Apply optional --label-filter and --capabilities to mirror worker filtering exactly.
4. SKIP the per-Run attempted map (it's non-deterministic across runs; not a stable picker decision).
5. Output an ordered table: Position | Bead ID | Priority | Updated | Status | Filter-decision | Why
   - 'Why' column explains each bead's pick-or-skip reason: 'next claim', 'eligible (rank N)', 'skipped: in cooldown until <RFC3339>', 'skipped: label_filter mismatch (bead labels=[a,b]; worker filter=[c])', 'skipped: capability mismatch (bead requires=[review]; worker has=[bead-attempt])', 'blocked: dep <id> open', etc.
6. Default --limit=10 (top of queue); --limit=0 = full queue.
7. Support --json for machine-readable output (operator scripts; comparison with actual claim events).

Implementation strategy: extract the picker's filter+sort logic from ExecuteBeadWorker.nextCandidate into a reusable function in cli/internal/agent (or cli/internal/bead) — call it 'PreviewQueue(filters PickerFilters) []QueueEntry'. The worker's nextCandidate then calls PreviewQueue + filters out attempted; the new 'ddx work plan' command calls PreviewQueue directly.

This factoring is itself the key insight: the picker logic SHOULD be a pure function over (bead store, filters) returning an ordered list. The worker's role is just 'call picker, claim first, execute'. Separating preview from claim gives us the dry-run + makes the picker testable in isolation.

NON-SCOPE
- Modifying picker semantics (this is read-only preview)
- Predicting downstream effects (e.g., 'after I claim X, what's next?' — out of scope; operator can re-run plan after each claim)
- Server-side picker integration (rev 5 architecture: there is no server-side picker; this command queries the bead store directly)
- Removing the per-Run attempted map (that's owned by C7 ddx-06eb05d8)
- Making 'ddx work' itself accept --plan as a flag instead (subcommand is clearer scope)

INTERSECTION
- Pairs with 'ddx try <bead-id>' (ddx-f261d2a0): operator runs 'ddx work plan' to see ordering, then 'ddx try <id>' to dispatch deterministically.
- Picker priority bug bead ddx-9d55601f shipped diagnostic events at commit 80f51574 (picker.priority_skip, picker.claim_race). 'ddx work plan' is the COMPLEMENT: events show what HAPPENED; plan shows what WOULD happen.
- C8 (ddx-848069a3) relocates routing preflight; 'ddx work plan' should NOT call routing preflight (it's a 'show me the queue' query, not 'show me what could execute given routing health').","id":"ddx-bc6d36e0","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:cli","area:agent","kind:feature","reliability","observability","wave:0"],"notes":"Shipped at commit c0f95380: PreviewQueue + ddx work plan subcommand. 13 tests added.","parent":"ddx-e34994e2","priority":0,"status":"closed","title":"cli: add 'ddx work plan' — dry-run picker preview showing exact ordering ddx work would pick","updated_at":"2026-05-03T20:32:54.490420167Z"}

.ddx/executions/20260503T015741-138c36ea/manifest.json:13:    "description": "OBSERVED
Two ddx workers (one server-managed, one --local --harness=claude) repeatedly claim P2 beads while four ready P0 beads sit unclaimed at the top of the queue.

REPRO (2026-05-02)
- ddx bead ready returns (top of list):
  - ddx-dc157075 P0
  - ddx-a946c744 P0 (triaged but child ddx-aee651ec P0 ready)
  - ddx-aee651ec P0
  - ddx-29058e2a P0
- Workers claim:
  - ddx-6d132f62 P2 (Story 16)
  - ddx-a7e7a1a1 P2 (Story 15-3)
  - ddx-297f840c P2 (Story 6 frontend) — earlier worker
- The 4 P0 beads have NO blocker:
  - extra.execute-loop-retry-after: null
  - extra.execution-eligible: null (defaults to true)
  - extra.superseded-by: null
  - all deps closed
- They appear in ddx bead ready AND should appear in Store.ReadyExecution() with priority=0 sorted first.

CODE PATH (per investigation)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1145 nextCandidate → calls Store.ReadyExecution()
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1246 ReadyExecution → readyFiltered(true)
- store.go:1357 calls sortBeadsForQueue(ready) which sorts by priority asc (0 first; verified at store.go:1462, 1475)
- Loop returns first candidate not in attempted map (loop.go:1150-1158)

By design this MUST return P0 first. Yet workers are getting P2. Hypotheses:
1. The `attempted` map persists across what should be fresh worker runs (worker process lifecycle bug — possibly related to ddx-dc157075).
2. A label filter is silently set on the worker spec (the server-managed worker had empty harness/model — possibly empty label filter is treated as a regex that excludes some labels).
3. Two workers race on Claim() and the loser falls through, retrying nextCandidate which now sees the bead in attempted and skips to lower-priority.
4. The server-managed worker uses a different picker path than ExecuteBeadWorker.nextCandidate (e.g., workers.go has its own selection that ignores priority).
5. The P0 beads have a label/area excluded by some default filter the operator doesn't see.

INVESTIGATION NEEDED
- Trace each currently-running worker: print its full spec (harness, model, labelFilter, oncce, pollInterval).
- Print the result of ReadyExecution() on demand (add a `ddx server workers next-candidate <worker-id>` debug command, or run from REPL).
- Add structured event when a worker passes over a P0 to claim a P2 — log the reason (in attempted, label-filtered, claim race, etc.).
- Inspect cli/internal/server/workers.go for any divergence from execute_bead_loop.go's nextCandidate.

LIKELY RELATED
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive fix) — workers exiting prematurely and the persisted `attempted` map across restarts could explain skips.
- ddx-29058e2a (server flag-drop) — if the spec is corrupted en route, label filter could be set incorrectly.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Changing the priority sort order itself.
- Adding new picker dimensions (story affinity, area concurrency caps).
- Refactoring the picker into a service (separate concern).",

.ddx/executions/20260506T084744-1b7e7938/manifest.json:12:    "description": "PROBLEM
  The drain loop tracks attempted and hookFailed beads via ad-hoc in-memory maps rather than a structured Guard contract, making the loop body untestable in isolation and the skip policy implicit.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413 declares attempted := make(map[string]struct{}) and hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{}). Lines 534-544 manually clear both maps between sleep cycles. Lines 591-601 implement the two-strikes pre-claim hook policy by mutating these maps inline. nextCandidate (line 1216) accepts attempted as a parameter and checks membership at line 1223. No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Define Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go: Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, reason string).
  - Implement two concrete Guards: PreClaimGuard (wraps runtime.PreClaimHook two-strikes logic) and ComplexityGuard (wraps w.ComplexityGate nil-check).
  - Delete attempted and hookFailed maps from execute_bead_loop.go:403-413. Replace nextCandidate's attempted parameter with drain.result.Results slice (already populated per-iteration): filter beads whose ID appears in Results.
  - Pre-claim hook denial: call w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, 30s, ...) instead of recording in hookFailed.
  - Add TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips and TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce to a new cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Any change to SetExecutionCooldown duration policy (controlled by caller constants).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: guard denials should skip the candidate rather than wedge the queue.
- P2: each guard owns only its own skip policy.
- P4: a denied bead stays isolated from other beads.",

  The drain loop tracks attempted and hookFailed beads via ad-hoc in-memory maps rather than a structured Guard contract, making the loop body untestable in isolation and the skip policy implicit.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413 declares attempted := make(map[string]struct{}) and hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{}). Lines 534-544 manually clear both maps between sleep cycles. Lines 591-601 implement the two-strikes pre-claim hook policy by mutating these maps inline. nextCandidate (line 1216) accepts attempted as a parameter and checks membership at line 1223. No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Define Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go: Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, reason string).
  - Implement two concrete Guards: PreClaimGuard (wraps runtime.PreClaimHook two-strikes logic) and ComplexityGuard (wraps w.ComplexityGate nil-check).
  - Delete attempted and hookFailed maps from execute_bead_loop.go:403-413. Replace nextCandidate's attempted parameter with drain.result.Results slice (already populated per-iteration): filter beads whose ID appears in Results.
  - Pre-claim hook denial: call w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, 30s, ...) instead of recording in hookFailed.
  - Add TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips and TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce to a new cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Any change to SetExecutionCooldown duration policy (controlled by caller constants).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: guard denials should skip the candidate rather than wedge the queue.
- P2: each guard owns only its own skip policy.
- P4: a denied bead stays isolated from other beads.",

Reviewed and approved by user before any implementation beads are filed from the roadmap.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T02:14:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"4025192","closing_commit_sha":"fabf513096b522e921020e323ef1bf4a9cf36afa","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:43:27.404796076Z","description":"Operator-observed gap: when ddx-server restarts, in-flight worker subprocesses lose their connection to the orchestrator. The current model has two execution paths (--local vs server-spawned) that diverge in lifecycle management, configuration plumbing (the dropped-flags class — see ddx-29058e2a), and observability (server can see only what it spawned).

PROPOSED ARCHITECTURAL SHIFT
WORKERS ARE LONG-LIVED PROCESSES THAT PHONE HOME TO THE SERVER VIA API. --local mode collapses to "worker registers with itself" using an in-process implementation of the same API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

THE ADR EVALUATES AND CHOOSES AMONG

PROPOSAL A — Server orchestrates, workers are long-lived clients
- Worker subprocess starts (either via `ddx work` CLI or via server-spawned)
- Worker registers with server (via /api/workers/register, gets worker-id + heartbeat token)
- Worker polls /api/workers/<id>/next-bead via long-poll or websocket; receives bead spec from server
- Worker reports progress via /api/workers/<id>/event {kind, body}
- Worker reports completion via /api/workers/<id>/result {bead-id, status, evidence}
- If server restarts: worker's heartbeat fails → worker enters "disconnected" state, finishes current bead, reconnects on server return
- If worker dies: server's heartbeat-timeout reclaims the bead

PROPOSAL B — Server is optional sidecar, worker is autonomous
- Worker runs independently; no server required
- If a server is reachable (via .ddx/server.addr), worker emits events to it best-effort
- Server is a passive observer, not an orchestrator
- Restart-safe by construction (worker doesn't depend on server)
- Loses: server-driven label filters, dynamic priority overrides, centralized cancel

PROPOSAL C — Hybrid: workers are autonomous executors but yield orchestration to server when present
- Worker checks for .ddx/server.addr at startup
- If present: registers as managed; receives bead assignments from server's queue
- If absent or unreachable: runs autonomously using local bead store
- Server restart: managed workers detect via heartbeat failure; gracefully complete current bead, then either retry registration OR fall back to autonomous mode (configurable)

UNIFIED API CONTRACT (all proposals)

POST /api/workers/register { project_root, harness, model_pref, label_filter, capabilities[] } → { worker_id, session_token, heartbeat_interval_ms }
POST /api/workers/<id>/heartbeat { state: idle|claiming|executing|reviewing } → { server_command: continue|pause|drain|terminate }
GET  /api/workers/<id>/next-bead → { bead, attempt_id, base_rev } | { wait_for_seconds: N }
POST /api/workers/<id>/event { kind, body, bead_id } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/result { bead_id, attempt_id, status, evidence_dir, commit_sha } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/disconnect → 204 (graceful)

The existing --local mode collapses to: a worker that registers with itself (no server) using an in-process implementation of the API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

OUTSTANDING BEADS THIS ADR MUST RECONCILE (overlap analysis required in the ADR output)

ABSORBED (implementation subsumed by the new architecture; ADR explicitly closes these as superseded once roadmap lands):
- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): becomes the API request/response payload — the spec IS the wire format. Refactor concern dissolves.
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): ALREADY FIXED at commit 41cb762e via flat 30s poll-interval default + attempted-map reset. Whether the new architecture preserves these tactical fixes or replaces them via API-driven semantics needs an ADR decision.
- ddx-cfedee8e (escalation: wire ladder): the ladder becomes server-side orchestration logic, not loop-body logic. ADR must specify where ladder lives in the new shape.

CONFLICTS WITH IN-FLIGHT REFACTOR (refactor epic ddx-5cb6e6cd children) — ADR must resolve sequencing:
- ddx-c8f79963 (C5: no_changes adjudication move into try.Attempt): refactors the SAME code that the API transition refactors. Either land C5 first then transition, OR fold C5 into the transition.
- ddx-06eb05d8 (C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps): same surface; same choice.
- ddx-848069a3 (C8: routing preflight to Drain startup): related to "server-side preflight" decision in API design.
- ddx-9228a484 (C9: StopCondition + cost-cap, absorbs ddx-cfedee8e and ddx-dc157075): heavy overlap. ADR must decide whether StopCondition becomes a server-side concept, a worker-side concept, or both.
- ddx-387a0178 (C13: file/identifier rename + ddx try CLI command): the ADR's new package layout (cli/internal/agent/work/) may conflict with C13's rename plan. Coordinate.
- ddx-1d867ec1 (rename execute_bead_loop.go): same as C13.

PARALLEL-SAFE (no conflict; ADR notes them but doesn't reshape):
- ddx-5b3e57f4 (cooldown: distinguish disrupted from gave-up): the API contract makes disruption naturally observable (server sees worker disconnect → marks Disrupted). But the cooldown logic itself is independent. ADR should call out that the API design enables cleaner Disrupted classification.
- ddx-1e516bc9 (fizeau Execute upstream gap): independent of API design.
- ddx-4c51d33e LAYER 1, ddx-055e8d32 LAYER 2, ddx-5ae050dc LAYER 3 (GraphQL project scoping): all in cli/internal/server/graphql/. Independent of worker API.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker bug): already fixed.
- ddx-a946c744 + ddx-aee651ec + ddx-3e60fd84 + ddx-83440482 (REACH/checks): hook into pre_merge gate; needs to integrate with the new worker contract but doesn't block ADR.
- All Story 6/14/15/16 frontend + backend feature beads: independent.

SPEC UPDATES THIS ADR DRIVES (the ADR roadmap must include these as deliverables)

- New ADR document: docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- FEAT-006 (Agent Service) — major amendment: worker API contract section, --local-collapses-to-API explanation, deprecation of direct execution paths
- FEAT-010 (Executions) — amendment: worker lifecycle now spans across server restarts; "execution" becomes a bead-attempt regardless of which worker performs it
- SD-024 (Resolved Config / CLIOverrides) — possible amendment: if ResolvedConfig becomes part of the API request payload, SD-024 reshapes
- TD-030 (Backend interface + chaos contract) — cross-reference if any
- CONTRACT-003 (Fizeau Service) — no change required (fizeau is below the worker; the API is between worker and ddx-server, not worker and fizeau)

NOT IN SCOPE FOR THIS ADR
- Specific code changes (this TD documents the design; implementation is follow-up beads from the roadmap)
- Multi-server federation (each server owns its workers; cross-server is a separate concern)
- Auth beyond session tokens (the worker-server channel is local; no remote workers in v1)
- Reshaping the bead schema (out of scope; ADR-004 owns that)

OUTPUTS (TD deliverables)
- ADR document at docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- Updated FEAT-006 (or proposed amendments listed)
- Sequence diagrams for: register, claim, execute, restart-recovery, graceful-shutdown
- Compatibility table: which existing tests/behaviors break, which migrate cleanly
- Implementation roadmap: ordered list of beads to file as follow-ups
- Overlap reconciliation table: each overlapping bead (above) classified as ABSORBED / CONFLICT-RESOLVED / PARALLEL-SAFE with explicit rationale

SEQUENCING DIRECTIVE (per user direction, 2026-05-03)
- Should land BEFORE major refactor children that touch the loop body (C5, C7, C9) — otherwise they'd refactor against the OLD model and need re-doing
- Should NOT block routine bug fixes or feature beads

.ddx/beads.jsonl:286:{"acceptance":"1. cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go calls RunConflictRecovery (or an adapter in try/conflict_recovery.go) when the Outcome indicates a preserved ref is available.
2. execute_bead_loop.go:847 block replaced with try.Attempt delegation; no direct RunConflictRecovery call remains in the loop.
3. TestAttempt_ConflictRecovered_ReturnsMergedOutcome in cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go passes: stub executor returns a conflict report, Attempt returns Outcome with disposition Merged.
4. TestAttempt_ConflictUnresolvable_ReturnsPark in cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go passes: auto-recover returns error, Attempt returns OutcomePark.
5. TestAttempt_WrapsLegacyExecutor (existing) still passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/agent/try/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:00:49.057830205Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-358f2457","depends_on_id":"ddx-a921ff01","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:00:49Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
  The conflict-recovery state machine (auto-merge + focused-resolve + close-or-park) is partially extracted but the description cites only the pre-extraction loop lines, leaving the executor without a clear picture of the current API boundary.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go:118 defines RunConflictRecovery(ctx, ConflictRecoveryInput) ConflictRecoveryOutput — the C4 extraction already landed. The bead description still refers to the old inline branch. The caller site is cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 (loop branches on ShouldAttemptConflictRecovery, then calls RunConflictRecovery). The try.Attempt API (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:67, func Attempt(ctx, store, beadID, AttemptOpts) (Outcome, error)) does not yet call RunConflictRecovery; conflict recovery remains loop-owned.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Migrate RunConflictRecovery invocation from execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 into try.Attempt (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go) so Attempt's return Outcome already encodes conflict-recovery disposition.
  - Add try/conflict_recovery.go: thin adapter from ConflictRecoveryOutput to Outcome (Disposition=Merged → OutcomeSuccess, Park/NeedsHuman → OutcomePark).
  - Loop caller (execute_bead_loop.go:847) delegates to try.Attempt instead of calling RunConflictRecovery directly; removes the inline branch.
  - Add TestAttempt_ConflictRecovered_ReturnsMergedOutcome and TestAttempt_ConflictUnresolvable_ReturnsPark to cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Deleting the attempted/hookFailed maps (C7, ddx-06eb05d8).
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: fail-open at the layer boundary when conflict recovery is not applicable or cannot complete cleanly.
- P2: conflict recovery owns only its own conditions; the loop should delegate rather than infer recovery policy.
- P3: the worker panel should surface conflict-recovery transitions and any degraded path.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:298:{"acceptance":"1. **Root cause #1 fixed:** `attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}` is moved to AFTER the pre-claim hook succeeds. Regression test in `execute_bead_loop_test.go` asserts: with a fake `PreClaimHook` that fails on the first call and succeeds thereafter, the loop processes the same bead on the second iteration (not skipped because of stale `attempted` state).

2. **Root cause #2 fixed:** the post-`Execute` outcome-handling block (lines ~510-862) does not exit the loop on transient `Store.*` errors. Each `return result, err` in that block is converted to: log the error, emit a `kind:loop-error` bead event when possible, set the bead to a short cooldown, and `continue` to the next iteration. The only exception is context cancellation (`ctx.Err()`), which still terminates immediately.

3. **Regression tests for AC-2:** for each `Store.*` call in the outcome block (Unclaim, IncrNoChangesCount, adjudicateNoChanges, CloseWithEvidence on each path, Reopen on each path, SetExecutionCooldown on each path, late AppendEvent), a test injects a transient error from a fake store and asserts: (a) the loop continues, (b) a `kind:loop-error` event is recorded with the failing operation name, (c) `result.Failures++` advances.

4. **End-to-end reproduction guard:** an integration test seeds a bead.jsonl with 3 ready beads. The first returns `no_changes` (executor fake), the second returns `success`, the third returns `already_satisfied`. The loop runs to completion (all three processed) without exiting prematurely. With the pre-fix loop, this test fails on the first bead.

5. **Worker-state semantics preserved:** existing tests that assert worker.State transitions (`running` → `exited` / `failed` / `stopped` / `reaped`) still pass. The fix changes loop *advancement*, not terminal-state classification.

6. **Documented behavior:** `docs/agents/execute-loop.md` (or the closest existing doc) gains a "loop termination" section listing the only conditions that terminate the loop: context cancellation, `Once == true`, `PollInterval <= 0` AND `nextCandidate` returns no candidate, `RoutePreflight` rejected. Explicitly states that Store errors during outcome handling do NOT terminate the loop.

7. **Operator workaround retired:** the README's "if `ddx work` exits early, restart it" guidance (or equivalent) is removed; that workaround was masking this bug.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"1495ba706ffdc0e7bfb4833487ed068ffe732aeb","created_at":"2026-04-29T19:05:38.552407002Z","description":"## Symptom

`ddx work` (no `--once` flag, no `--poll-interval`, default mode) exits after processing exactly one bead instead of draining the queue. Reproduced four times today on `/home/erik/Projects/agent` with `--harness lmstudio` pinned (which sidesteps the unrelated `ddx-c7081f89` server-worker tier-walk issue):

| Run | Outcome on first bead | Worker terminal state | Queue still non-empty? |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | `already_satisfied` (`agent-4aa3584e` adjudicated satisfied) | `already_satisfied` / `exited` | yes — 2 ready beads remained |
| 3 | pre-claim hook rejected (diverged branch) | `exited`, attempts=0 | yes |
| 4 | pre-claim hook rejected (diverged branch) | `exited`, attempts=0 | yes |
| 5 | `no_changes` from agent (`agent-6c7a4c11` declined as epic; bead left **claimed**) | `exited`, attempts=1 | yes — 5 ready beads remained |

In all four runs the loop should have continued to the next ready bead. Per `agent/.claude/skills/ddx/reference/work.md`, only `success`/`already_satisfied` close the bead and `no_changes`/`land_conflict`/`*_failed` unclaim and continue. None of these outcomes is documented or expected to be loop-terminal.

## Root cause #1 (verified)

`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:382`:

```go
attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}

// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.
// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop
// continues (ctx is not cancelled).
if runtime.PreClaimHook != nil {
    if hookErr := runtime.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {
        // ... log + emit ...
        continue
    }
}
```

The bead is added to `attempted` **before** the pre-claim hook runs. On hook failure (e.g. diverged branch), the loop `continue`s but the bead stays in `attempted`. Next iteration, `nextCandidate()` (line 924) skips the still-ready bead because it's in `attempted`, returns `ok=false`, and the loop exits at line 374 because `PollInterval <= 0`. Single-shot exit with `attempts=0`.

**Reproduces:** runs 3 and 4 above.

## Root cause #2 (suspected — needs targeted instrumentation)

Runs 2 and 5 reached `Execute()` and got a real report back. Worker log shows the entry line `▶ <bead>: <title>` but no closing `✓ <bead> → <result>` line (which is written at line 909). That means the loop exited somewhere between line 488 (`Executor.Execute`) and line 909 (the final summary log).

Between those bounds, **at least 16 distinct `return result, err` points** trigger if any `Store.*` operation returns an error: `Unclaim` (711, 700), `IncrNoChangesCount` (717), `adjudicateNoChanges` (721), `CloseWithEvidence` (520, 615, 694, 739), `Reopen` (635, 669), `SetExecutionCooldown` (749, 791, 803, 835, 844), late `AppendEvent` (862). Any single transient store error during outcome handling kills the worker.

For run 5 specifically, `agent-6c7a4c11` is now `Status: in_progress` with `Owner: ddx, Machine: sindri`. The bead was **never unclaimed** despite the agent returning `no_changes` — meaning the loop exited on or before line 711 (`w.Store.Unclaim`) failing. Investigation should look at what error `Unclaim` would return when called against a bead the same worker claimed seconds earlier; possible candidates: tracker-write race with concurrent worker, bead.jsonl re-write during a fetch by the pre-claim hook on a later iteration that already happened (no — pre-claim is pre-claim, not post-execute), or a malformed bead row that fails store-side validation.

Independent of the specific store error: the structural pattern of "any Store.* error → exit the entire loop" is the wrong default. A store error on outcome handling should at most park the bead and continue to the next, never terminate the worker.

## Why this is loadbearing

The loop is the queue-drain primitive. Every observed failure-to-drain in the last 24 hours of operator complaints traces back to this — beads that should have been the worker's second/third/fourth attempt never got picked up because the worker exited after one attempt, and the operator manually restarts the drain (sometimes after a long delay). The visible queue depth is misleading: half the "ready" beads are sitting behind a worker that quietly died.

## In-scope files

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` — fixes for both root causes
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go` — regression tests for each path

## Out of scope

- Anything in the `agent` SDK (`/home/erik/Projects/agent`). The loop and all its store interactions are owned by ddx.
- The pre-claim hook's divergence-detection logic itself — that's correct behavior; the bug is how the loop reacts to the hook's signal.
- Adaptive min-tier / tier-walk machinery (`ddx-c7081f89`, `ddx-aec9d68c`) — independent.

## Anti-goals

- Do **not** swallow store errors silently. They must be surfaced (event + log) but should not kill the worker on transient/per-bead failures.
- Do **not** convert the loop's Once/PollInterval semantics — they're correct.
- Do **not** change worker terminal-state semantics (`exited`/`failed`/`stopped`/`reaped`).","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_model":"sonnet","fallback_chain":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-29T20:58:01.9171561Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude model=sonnet"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260429T203909-e46a1ac0","harness":"claude","model":"sonnet","input_tokens":92,"output_tokens":47611,"total_tokens":47703,"cost_usd":3.409703350000001,"duration_ms":1131913,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-04-29T20:58:02.023531375Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=47703 cost_usd=3.4097 model=sonnet"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"sonnet","resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"2026-04-29T20:58:06.183307339Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=claude model=sonnet"},{"actor":"erik","body":"Diff only contains execution evidence (manifest.json + result.json) under .ddx/executions/. ExecuteBeadWorker.Run() is a 700-line function with 24 returns: 4 legitimate, 1 the poll-interval-zero exit, 1 the routing-preflight-kills-worker exit, and 18 store-error escalations from the same handleOutcomeStoreError pattern repeated 14 times. The user undercounted the smell by ~5×. The implicit state machine in the loop is real and coherent (Pick → Gate → Claim → Execute → Classify → Reviewing → Terminal) but written as conditional soup instead of explicit state.

## Three-package split aligned to FEAT-010

  cli/internal/agent/run/   — Layer 1: invocation atom (existing dispatch + runner)
  cli/internal/agent/try/   — Layer 2: bead attempt (worktree, prompt, single Execute, Land, conflict-recovery, post-merge review, triage)
  cli/internal/agent/work/  — Layer 3: queue drain (pick, claim, heartbeat, drain-loop control, stop conditions)

try.Attempt(beadID) returns sealed Outcome { Disposition (enum), Cooldown, ParkReason, RecordEvents }. Loop becomes ~30 lines: pick → guard → claim → attemptWithHeartbeat → applyOutcome. Single commitOutcome helper deletes the 18 store-error returns.

## Ordering: refactor-LAST per user decision

In-flight Group A beads (c6e3db02 rate-limit, b24e9630 no_changes, cfedee8e ladder wire, a8718bec cost-cap reviewer) land first against current code. dc157075 tactical worker-stay-alive lands first as operational bridge. THIS refactor lands last and absorbs all intervening behavior into the new architecture. Per-child ACs explicitly call out which in-flight bead's behavior each child must preserve.

## Naming

Package = try (not try_bead — Go idiom). Function = try.Attempt(beadID). Loop = work.Worker.Drain. CLI surface (ddx run / try / work) already correct; C13 also ADDS ddx try as a new CLI command (FEAT-010 named it but it doesn't exist yet).

Operators have to manually relaunch, defeating the point of a long-running drain.

## Root causes (from sub-agent investigation)

1. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:440-441**: when nextCandidate returns no eligible bead AND runtime.PollInterval <= 0, the loop returns immediately. Combined with the per-Run 'attempted' map (line 388, 476), once each currently-ready bead has been *attempted once* the loop exits even though those same beads (or new arrivals) are still queue-ready.

2. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:483-508**: routing-preflight rejection on a single bead exits the entire loop ('return result, nil'), abandoning all other ready beads.

3. **Default --poll-interval=0 everywhere**: cli/cmd/work.go:66, cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1568, cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:115, cli/internal/server/server.go:2281. All three recent server-spawned workers in .ddx/workers/ show poll_interval: '0s', so cause #1 fires the first empty poll.

## Fix shape (locked decisions baked in)

- Flip default --poll-interval from 0 → **30s** everywhere (CLI + server adapter). Operator can override with --once for one-shot or explicit --poll-interval=N.
- Reset per-Run 'attempted'/'hookFailed' maps when the loop falls through to 'no candidate', before sleeping. Prevents the 'attempted-once = exit' trap.
- Convert preflight early-exit (483-508) to per-bead 'continue' so one bead's routing failure doesn't abandon the rest.
- Add structured exit_reason to loop.end event; reserve 'providers_exhausted' slot for ddx-aede917d (quota-pause work).
- Server-managed workers expose 'no_ready_work' as a **transient 'running (idle)' substate**, not terminal. Worker stays alive; UI shows running (idle).
- Preserve --once and explicit --poll-interval=0 for back-compat (legacy 'drain-and-exit' semantics).

Acceptable exit conditions remain (per existing beads):
- SIGTERM / SIGINT (operator-driven)
- All providers durably out (per ddx-aede917d quota pause/resume — workers pause+probe, not exit)
- No providers configured at all (fatal config error)
- --once explicitly requested

(a) What is done

    (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:492-559). The early-return
    path only fires for the explicit opt-out (--once or
    --poll-interval=0).

    exit (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:608-651). The bead is

    next candidate (still honouring --once when set).

Status
------
The fix for both root causes is present in HEAD via commit d57f1ddf
("fix: execute-loop exits after one bead due to attempted tracking and
fatal store errors [ddx-37cdb43a]"), which was merged into main as
7290916b before this worktree's base-rev (1495ba70).

Verification against AC
-----------------------
AC1 (root cause #1, attempted-after-hook): satisfied.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:378 declares `hookFailed`.
  - lines 447-464: pre-claim hook failure routes through hookFailed first;
    `attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}` only fires after a SECOND failure.
  - line 466: attempted is set unconditionally only AFTER the hook block exits
    successfully.
  - Regression test: TestExecuteBeadLoop_PreClaimHookRetryOnTransientFailure
    (and friends) in execute_bead_loop_test.go.

AC2 (root cause #2, store errors are non-terminal): satisfied.
  - handleOutcomeStoreError defined at execute_bead_loop.go:83. Logs, emits
    kind:loop-error, sets StoreErrorCooldown, increments result.Failures,
    returns true to signal continue. ctx.Err() is the only termination path.
  - Every Store.* call in the outcome block (Unclaim, IncrNoChangesCount,
    adjudicateNoChanges, CloseWithEvidence on each path, Reopen on each
    path, SetExecutionCooldown on each path) is wrapped with the helper.
  - The late AppendEvent at ~line 1013 is non-terminal too (logs +
    StoreErrorCooldown + continue).

AC3 (per-call regression tests): satisfied — table-driven tests in
execute_bead_loop_test.go cover each Store.* path.

AC4 (e2e 3-bead drain guard): satisfied — drain regression test exists.

AC5 (worker-state semantics preserved): satisfied — the fix changes loop
advancement only.

AC6 (documented termination conditions): satisfied — docs/agent-execute.md
line 80 "Loop termination conditions" lists the only terminating
conditions and explicitly states Store errors do NOT terminate the loop.

AC7 (operator workaround retired): no "restart it" guidance remains in
README or docs; no further removal needed.

Verification run
----------------
`cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/ -run "TestExecuteBeadLoop|TestRun|HookRetry|StoreError" -count=1`
→ ok (11s).

Why this attempt is no_changes
------------------------------
The bead was reopened with a REVIEW:BLOCK note observing the previous
attempt's diff "only contained execution evidence". That is consistent with
an attempt whose worktree was already past the fix: there was nothing
left to commit. Adding code now would either duplicate the existing
implementation or churn it without changing behaviour. Closing the bead is
the operator action; this iteration confirms the AC are met.

.ddx/executions/20260506T055007-bd59640c/manifest.json:12:    "description": "PROBLEM
  The drain loop tracks attempted and hookFailed beads via ad-hoc in-memory maps rather than a structured Guard contract, making the loop body untestable in isolation and the skip policy implicit.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413 declares attempted := make(map[string]struct{}) and hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{}). Lines 534-544 manually clear both maps between sleep cycles. Lines 591-601 implement the two-strikes pre-claim hook policy by mutating these maps inline. nextCandidate (line 1216) accepts attempted as a parameter and checks membership at line 1223. No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Define Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go: Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, reason string).
  - Implement two concrete Guards: PreClaimGuard (wraps runtime.PreClaimHook two-strikes logic) and ComplexityGuard (wraps w.ComplexityGate nil-check).
  - Delete attempted and hookFailed maps from execute_bead_loop.go:403-413. Replace nextCandidate's attempted parameter with drain.result.Results slice (already populated per-iteration): filter beads whose ID appears in Results.
  - Pre-claim hook denial: call w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, 30s, ...) instead of recording in hookFailed.
  - Add TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips and TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce to a new cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Any change to SetExecutionCooldown duration policy (controlled by caller constants).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: guard denials should skip the candidate rather than wedge the queue.
- P2: each guard owns only its own skip policy.
- P4: a denied bead stays isolated from other beads.",
.ddx/executions/20260506T055007-bd59640c/manifest.json:13:    "acceptance": "1. Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go with Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, string) signature.
2. PreClaimGuard and ComplexityGuard implement Guard; loop body passes them to nextCandidate.
3. attempted and hookFailed maps deleted from cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413; no references remain.
4. nextCandidate signature no longer accepts attempted map; filters via drain.result.Results instead.
5. Pre-claim hook denial calls w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown with 30s cooldown; hookFailed logic removed.
6. TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips in cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go: two consecutive Allow calls with hook error → second returns false.
7. TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce: nil ComplexityGate → one warning, allow proceeds.
8. Existing TestPreClaimHookDivergedSkipsBead in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_preclaim_test.go still passes.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/agent/work/... green.
10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.",

.ddx/executions/20260505T103941-e21c5fa9/manifest.json:12:    "description": "PROBLEM
  The conflict-recovery state machine (auto-merge + focused-resolve + close-or-park) is partially extracted but the description cites only the pre-extraction loop lines, leaving the executor without a clear picture of the current API boundary.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go:118 defines RunConflictRecovery(ctx, ConflictRecoveryInput) ConflictRecoveryOutput — the C4 extraction already landed. The bead description still refers to the old inline branch. The caller site is cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 (loop branches on ShouldAttemptConflictRecovery, then calls RunConflictRecovery). The try.Attempt API (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:67, func Attempt(ctx, store, beadID, AttemptOpts) (Outcome, error)) does not yet call RunConflictRecovery; conflict recovery remains loop-owned.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Migrate RunConflictRecovery invocation from execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 into try.Attempt (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go) so Attempt's return Outcome already encodes conflict-recovery disposition.
  - Add try/conflict_recovery.go: thin adapter from ConflictRecoveryOutput to Outcome (Disposition=Merged → OutcomeSuccess, Park/NeedsHuman → OutcomePark).
  - Loop caller (execute_bead_loop.go:847) delegates to try.Attempt instead of calling RunConflictRecovery directly; removes the inline branch.
  - Add TestAttempt_ConflictRecovered_ReturnsMergedOutcome and TestAttempt_ConflictUnresolvable_ReturnsPark to cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Deleting the attempted/hookFailed maps (C7, ddx-06eb05d8).
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: fail-open at the layer boundary when conflict recovery is not applicable or cannot complete cleanly.
- P2: conflict recovery owns only its own conditions; the loop should delegate rather than infer recovery policy.
- P3: the worker panel should surface conflict-recovery transitions and any degraded path.
- P4: a bad bead X must not spill into bead Y; conflict recovery stays per-bead.",

.ddx/executions/20260503T025612-a3a8cb42/manifest.json:13:    "description": "Workers exit prematurely even when ready beads remain in the queue. Operators have to manually relaunch, defeating the point of a long-running drain.

## Root causes (from sub-agent investigation)

1. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:440-441**: when nextCandidate returns no eligible bead AND runtime.PollInterval <= 0, the loop returns immediately. Combined with the per-Run 'attempted' map (line 388, 476), once each currently-ready bead has been *attempted once* the loop exits even though those same beads (or new arrivals) are still queue-ready.

2. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:483-508**: routing-preflight rejection on a single bead exits the entire loop ('return result, nil'), abandoning all other ready beads.

3. **Default --poll-interval=0 everywhere**: cli/cmd/work.go:66, cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1568, cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:115, cli/internal/server/server.go:2281. All three recent server-spawned workers in .ddx/workers/ show poll_interval: '0s', so cause #1 fires the first empty poll.

## Fix shape (locked decisions baked in)

- Flip default --poll-interval from 0 → **30s** everywhere (CLI + server adapter). Operator can override with --once for one-shot or explicit --poll-interval=N.
- Reset per-Run 'attempted'/'hookFailed' maps when the loop falls through to 'no candidate', before sleeping. Prevents the 'attempted-once = exit' trap.
- Convert preflight early-exit (483-508) to per-bead 'continue' so one bead's routing failure doesn't abandon the rest.
- Add structured exit_reason to loop.end event; reserve 'providers_exhausted' slot for ddx-aede917d (quota-pause work).
- Server-managed workers expose 'no_ready_work' as a **transient 'running (idle)' substate**, not terminal. Worker stays alive; UI shows running (idle).
- Preserve --once and explicit --poll-interval=0 for back-compat (legacy 'drain-and-exit' semantics).

Acceptable exit conditions remain (per existing beads):
- SIGTERM / SIGINT (operator-driven)
- All providers durably out (per ddx-aede917d quota pause/resume — workers pause+probe, not exit)
- No providers configured at all (fatal config error)
- --once explicitly requested
- --poll-interval=0 explicitly requested",
.ddx/executions/20260503T025612-a3a8cb42/manifest.json:14:    "acceptance": "1. Default --poll-interval = 30s in all four call sites: cli/cmd/work.go:66, cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1568, cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:115, cli/internal/server/server.go:2281. 2. execute_bead_loop.go:440-441 no longer returns early when poll-interval > 0; sleeps + retries instead. Per-Run 'attempted'/'hookFailed' maps reset on no-candidate path. 3. execute_bead_loop.go:483-508 preflight rejection becomes per-bead continue, not loop exit. 4. loop.end event includes structured exit_reason field. Recognized values include 'sigterm', 'sigint', 'fatal_config', 'once_complete', 'explicit_poll_zero'; 'providers_exhausted' slot reserved for ddx-aede917d. 5. Server-managed workers report no_ready_work as transient (worker stays in 'running' state with 'idle' substate); existing 'exited' status reserved for true exits per AC #4. 6. Tests: TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue (poll-interval > 0; verify loop stays alive across N empty polls); TestLoop_PreflightRejectionContinues (one bead's preflight failure doesn't abandon siblings); TestLoop_OnceFlagStillExits (back-compat); TestLoop_ExplicitPollZeroExits (back-compat). 7. Manual verification: spawn worker via 'ddx work --harness claude'; let queue drain; verify worker stays running > 5 min after queue empty (vs current behavior of immediate exit). 8. Update FEAT-006 / FEAT-010 docs noting the new long-running default + the explicit opt-out flags.",

.ddx/executions/20260502T205137-13829788/prompt.md:5:Workers exit prematurely even when ready beads remain in the queue. Operators have to manually relaunch, defeating the point of a long-running drain.

## Root causes (from sub-agent investigation)

1. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:440-441**: when nextCandidate returns no eligible bead AND runtime.PollInterval <= 0, the loop returns immediately. Combined with the per-Run 'attempted' map (line 388, 476), once each currently-ready bead has been *attempted once* the loop exits even though those same beads (or new arrivals) are still queue-ready.

2. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:483-508**: routing-preflight rejection on a single bead exits the entire loop ('return result, nil'), abandoning all other ready beads.

3. **Default --poll-interval=0 everywhere**: cli/cmd/work.go:66, cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1568, cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:115, cli/internal/server/server.go:2281. All three recent server-spawned workers in .ddx/workers/ show poll_interval: '0s', so cause #1 fires the first empty poll.

## Fix shape (locked decisions baked in)

- Flip default --poll-interval from 0 → **30s** everywhere (CLI + server adapter). Operator can override with --once for one-shot or explicit --poll-interval=N.
- Reset per-Run 'attempted'/'hookFailed' maps when the loop falls through to 'no candidate', before sleeping. Prevents the 'attempted-once = exit' trap.
- Convert preflight early-exit (483-508) to per-bead 'continue' so one bead's routing failure doesn't abandon the rest.
- Add structured exit_reason to loop.end event; reserve 'providers_exhausted' slot for ddx-aede917d (quota-pause work).
- Server-managed workers expose 'no_ready_work' as a **transient 'running (idle)' substate**, not terminal. Worker stays alive; UI shows running (idle).
- Preserve --once and explicit --poll-interval=0 for back-compat (legacy 'drain-and-exit' semantics).

Acceptable exit conditions remain (per existing beads):
- SIGTERM / SIGINT (operator-driven)
- All providers durably out (per ddx-aede917d quota pause/resume — workers pause+probe, not exit)
- No providers configured at all (fatal config error)
- --once explicitly requested
- --poll-interval=0 explicitly requested

Status
------
The fix for both root causes is present in HEAD via commit d57f1ddf
("fix: execute-loop exits after one bead due to attempted tracking and
fatal store errors [ddx-37cdb43a]"), which was merged into main as
7290916b before this worktree's base-rev (1495ba70).

Verification against AC
-----------------------
AC1 (root cause #1, attempted-after-hook): satisfied.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:378 declares `hookFailed`.
  - lines 447-464: pre-claim hook failure routes through hookFailed first;
    `attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}` only fires after a SECOND failure.
  - line 466: attempted is set unconditionally only AFTER the hook block exits
    successfully.
  - Regression test: TestExecuteBeadLoop_PreClaimHookRetryOnTransientFailure
    (and friends) in execute_bead_loop_test.go.

AC2 (root cause #2, store errors are non-terminal): satisfied.
  - handleOutcomeStoreError defined at execute_bead_loop.go:83. Logs, emits
    kind:loop-error, sets StoreErrorCooldown, increments result.Failures,
    returns true to signal continue. ctx.Err() is the only termination path.
  - Every Store.* call in the outcome block (Unclaim, IncrNoChangesCount,
    adjudicateNoChanges, CloseWithEvidence on each path, Reopen on each
    path, SetExecutionCooldown on each path) is wrapped with the helper.
  - The late AppendEvent at ~line 1013 is non-terminal too (logs +
    StoreErrorCooldown + continue).

AC3 (per-call regression tests): satisfied — table-driven tests in
execute_bead_loop_test.go cover each Store.* path.

AC4 (e2e 3-bead drain guard): satisfied — drain regression test exists.

AC5 (worker-state semantics preserved): satisfied — the fix changes loop
advancement only.

AC6 (documented termination conditions): satisfied — docs/agent-execute.md
line 80 "Loop termination conditions" lists the only terminating
conditions and explicitly states Store errors do NOT terminate the loop.

AC7 (operator workaround retired): no "restart it" guidance remains in
README or docs; no further removal needed.

Verification run
----------------
`cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/ -run "TestExecuteBeadLoop|TestRun|HookRetry|StoreError" -count=1`
→ ok (11s).

an attempt whose worktree was already past the fix: there was nothing
left to commit. Adding code now would either duplicate the existing
implementation or churn it without changing behaviour. Closing the bead is
the operator action; this iteration confirms the AC are met.",

package cmd

func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentCommand() *cobra.Command {
	cmd := &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "agent",
		Short: "Invoke AI agents with harness dispatch, quorum, and session logging",
		Long: `Unified interface for dispatching work to AI coding agents.

Supports multiple harnesses (codex, claude, gemini, etc.) with output capture,
token tracking, session logging, and multi-agent quorum.

The embedded DDx agent harness is named 'agent' and is always available without
installing external binaries. Use --harness agent or --profile cheap to route to it.

Profile routing (--profile default|cheap|fast|smart) selects the best available harness
and model automatically. Workflow tools should prefer --profile over --harness to
stay decoupled from harness installation details.

Examples:
  ddx agent run --profile default --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --profile cheap --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --profile smart --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --profile smart --model gpt-5.4   # explicit override; avoid by default
  ddx agent run --harness agent --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --harness codex --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --quorum majority --harnesses codex,claude --prompt task.md
  ddx agent list
  ddx agent capabilities agent
  ddx agent capabilities codex
  ddx agent doctor
  ddx agent log`,
		PersistentPreRunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
			serverreg.TryRegisterAsync(f.WorkingDir)
			return nil
		},
		Run: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
			_ = cmd.Help()
		},
	}

	cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentRunCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentCondenseCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentListCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentCapabilitiesCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentDoctorCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentLogCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentBenchmarkCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentUsageCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentReplayCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentExecuteBeadCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentExecuteLoopCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentExecutionsCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentWorkersCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentCatalogCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentProvidersCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentModelsCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentCheckCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentRouteStatusCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentMetricsCommand())

	return cmd
}

func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentRunCommand() *cobra.Command {
	cmd := &cobra.Command{
		Use:          "run",
		Short:        "Invoke an agent with a prompt",
		SilenceUsage: true,
		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
			promptFile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("prompt")
			promptText, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("text")
			harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
			model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
			profile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("profile")
			effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
			timeoutStr, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("timeout")
			quorum, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("quorum")
			harnesses, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harnesses")
			asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
			outputFmt, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("output")
			worktreeName, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("worktree")
			permissions, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("permissions")
			compare, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("compare")
			sandbox, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("sandbox")
			keepSandbox, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("keep-sandbox")
			postRun, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("post-run")
			arms, _ := cmd.Flags().GetStringArray("arm")

			var timeout time.Duration
			if timeoutStr != "" {
				var err error
				timeout, err = time.ParseDuration(timeoutStr)
				if err != nil {
					return fmt.Errorf("invalid timeout: %w", err)
				}
			}

			// Resolve project root (--project flag > DDX_PROJECT_ROOT > CWD git root)
			projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
			workDir := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
			if worktreeName != "" {
				wtPath, err := resolveWorktree(workDir, worktreeName)
				if err != nil {
					return fmt.Errorf("worktree: %w", err)
				}
				workDir = wtPath
			}

			// Read prompt from stdin if neither file nor text provided
			prompt := promptText
			promptSource := "inline"
			if prompt == "" && promptFile == "" {
				// Check if stdin has data
				stat, _ := os.Stdin.Stat()
				if (stat.Mode() & os.ModeCharDevice) == 0 {
					data, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)
					if err != nil {
						return fmt.Errorf("reading stdin: %w", err)
					}
					prompt = string(data)
					promptSource = "stdin"
				}
			} else if promptFile != "" {
				promptSource = promptFile
			}

			// Comparison mode
			if compare {
				var harnessNames []string
				armModels := map[int]string{}
				armLabels := map[int]string{}

ID:       ddx-06eb05d8
Title:    C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps + pick() derives from drain.result
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 1
Labels:   phase:2, refactor, kind:refactor, triage:no-changes-unjustified
Parent:   ddx-5cb6e6cd
Deps:     ddx-b669bb9f
Desc:     PROBLEM
  The drain loop tracks attempted and hookFailed beads via ad-hoc in-memory maps rather than a structured Guard contract, making the loop body untestable in isolation and the skip policy implicit.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413 declares attempted := make(map[string]struct{}) and hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{}). Lines 534-544 manually clear both maps between sleep cycles. Lines 591-601 implement the two-strikes pre-claim hook policy by mutating these maps inline. nextCandidate (line 1216) accepts attempted as a parameter and checks membership at line 1223. No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Define Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go: Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, reason string).
  - Implement two concrete Guards: PreClaimGuard (wraps runtime.PreClaimHook two-strikes logic) and ComplexityGuard (wraps w.ComplexityGate nil-check).
  - Delete attempted and hookFailed maps from execute_bead_loop.go:403-413. Replace nextCandidate's attempted parameter with drain.result.Results slice (already populated per-iteration): filter beads whose ID appears in Results.
  - Pre-claim hook denial: call w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, 30s, ...) instead of recording in hookFailed.
  - Add TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips and TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce to a new cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Any change to SetExecutionCooldown duration policy (controlled by caller constants).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: guard denials should skip the candidate rather than wedge the queue.
- P2: each guard owns only its own skip policy.
- P4: a denied bead stays isolated from other beads.
Accept:   1. Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go with Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, string) signature.
2. PreClaimGuard and ComplexityGuard implement Guard; loop body passes them to nextCandidate.
3. attempted and hookFailed maps deleted from cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413; no references remain.
4. nextCandidate signature no longer accepts attempted map; filters via drain.result.Results instead.
5. Pre-claim hook denial calls w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown with 30s cooldown; hookFailed logic removed.
6. TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips in cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go: two consecutive Allow calls with hook error → second returns false.
7. TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce: nil ComplexityGate → one warning, allow proceeds.
8. Existing TestPreClaimHookDivergedSkipsBead in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_preclaim_test.go still passes.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/agent/work/... green.
10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

   606	3. **server: operator-cancel** — `/api/beads/<id>/cancel` writes `extra.cancel-requested: true`; worker mid-attempt poll (10s default) checks bead `extra` and aborts at next safe point with `preserved_for_review` reason `operator_cancel`; idempotency via `cancel-honored: true`. ~150 LOC.
   607	
   608	4. **CLI: deprecate `--local`** — flag becomes no-op with deprecation warning; update CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, getting-started, and the cobra help text. Existing tests asserting `--local`-specific behavior get rewritten or deleted. (Codex notes the sweep is larger than 50 LOC: `cli/cmd/work_test.go`, `agent_execute_loop_test.go`, `zero_config_work_test.go`, `skills/ddx/reference/work.md`, demos.) ~150 LOC + doc edits.
   609	
   610	5. **server: derived-view GraphQL + UI workers panel** — schema fields: `workers { id, project, harness, state, last_event_at, mirror_failures_count, had_dropped_backfill, current_bead, current_attempt }`; UI surfaces freshness indicators + duplicate-worker display + the "trusted-peer reported, not authoritative" labeling. Existing workers panel migrated. ~400 LOC frontend + ~100 LOC backend.
   611	
   612	6. **`ddx agent doctor` migration** — read worker state from server's runtime registry when available; fall back to `.ddx/workers/` on-disk files when no server. The on-disk format stays as the fallback-source-of-truth for one alpha release lag, then deprecated. ~150 LOC.
   613	
   614	7. **server-spawn path migration** — `cli/internal/server/workers.go` and `handleStartExecuteLoopWorker` switch from hand-marshalled spec to `exec ddx work` with env-vars. The legacy spec serialization deletes when this lands. ~200 LOC + test updates.
   615	
   616	8. **FEAT-006 amendment** — replace the rev 3 worker-contract section with rev 5's autonomous-default + best-effort-mirror description. Drop session-token/heartbeat/`next-bead`/result/disconnect contract; add register/event/backfill/cancel + freshness state. ~200 lines docs.
   617	
   618	9. **acceptance + soak** — uses `ddx-50da9674` clean fixture repo. Multi-worker drain WITH server; restart server mid-flight; verify worker continues + reconnects + backfills. Then drain with NO server; verify identical behavior. Operator-cancel test (write marker, observe `preserved_for_review` with `operator_cancel` reason within SLA). Soak: ≥1 week of normal operator use. Final bead.
   619	
   620	Plus one COORDINATION bead, NOT new work but a parking-lot for the
   621	C7 conflict:
   622	
   623	10. **coordinate: C7 (Guard contract, delete attempted map) with rev 5 worker** — either C7 lands first and rev 5 implementation uses Guard-derived exclusion, OR C7 stays parked until rev 5's worker code defines its replacement for the per-Run attempted map. Decision documented; no LOC.
   624	
   625	Total estimated effort: 2-4 weeks (vs 1-2 weeks for rev 4's understated 5-bead roadmap, vs 8-10 weeks for rev 3's design).
   626

30	Unlike 'ddx bead ready', plan applies the picker's filters:
    31	  - Execution eligibility (cooldown, superseded, execution-eligible flag)
    32	  - Optional label-filter intersection (--label-filter)
    33	  - Optional capabilities constraint (--capabilities; currently a no-op)
    34	
    35	The per-Run attempted map is intentionally excluded: it is non-deterministic
    36	across runs and is not part of the stable picker decision surface.
    37	
    38	Columns:
    39	  POS      — 1-based pick order
    40	  ID       — bead ID
    41	  PRI      — priority (0 = highest)
    42	  UPDATED  — last updated timestamp (RFC3339)

data.text:
Closed `ddx-71603225` on path A.

Evidence:
- The ADR already records C7 as a coordination item with path A semantics, including Guard-derived exclusion and the fact that C7 is parked until coordinated: [ADR-022 worker architecture](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-71603225-20260506T094409-48a37da0/docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-022-worker-client-server-architecture.md#L572) and [the step-10 roadmap entry](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-71603225-20260506T094409-48a37da0/docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-022-worker-client-server-architecture.md#L623).
- The worker plan docs already state the per-Run attempted map is intentionally excluded from the stable picker surface: [cli/cmd/work_plan.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-71603225-20260506T094409-48a37da0/cli/cmd/work_plan.go#L35).
- The same ADR preserves the regression constraints the bead called out: stay-alive defaults from `41cb762e` and picker diagnostic events from `80f51574` remain intact: [ADR-022 worker architecture](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-71603225-20260506T094409-48a37da0/docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-022-worker-client-server-architecture.md#L574).

No repository files changed in this worktree, so there was nothing to commit here.
